tEn: A General Language Assistant as a Laboratory for Alignment
tZh: 通用语言助手作为对齐研究的实验室
sEn: Simple alignment techniques like prompting improve with model scale and generalize across evaluations without harming performance.
sZh: 简单的对齐技术（如提示）随模型规模扩大而效果提升，且能泛化到多种对齐评估中，同时不损害性能。
absEn: Given the broad capabilities of large language models, it should be possible to work towards a general-purpose, text-based assistant that is aligned with human values, meaning that it is helpful, honest, and harmless. As an initial foray in this direction we study simple baseline techniques and evaluations, such as prompting. We find that the benefits from modest interventions increase with model size, generalize to a variety of alignment evaluations, and do not compromise the performance of large models. Next we investigate scaling trends for several training objectives relevant to alignment, comparing imitation learning, binary discrimination, and ranked preference modeling. We find that ranked preference modeling performs much better than imitation learning, and often scales more favorably with model size. In contrast, binary discrimination typically performs and scales very similarly to imitation learning. Finally we study a `preference model pre-training' stage of training, with the goal of improving sample efficiency when finetuning on human preferences.
absZh: 鉴于大型语言模型的广泛能力，我们应致力于开发一种通用、基于文本的助手，使其与人类价值观对齐，即有用、诚实且无害。作为这一方向的初步探索，我们研究了简单的基线技术和评估方法，如提示。我们发现，适度的干预措施带来的益处随模型规模增大而增加，能泛化到多种对齐评估中，且不会损害大型模型的性能。接下来，我们研究了与对齐相关的几种训练目标的扩展趋势，比较了模仿学习、二元判别和排序偏好建模。我们发现，排序偏好建模的表现远优于模仿学习，且通常随模型规模扩展更有利。相比之下，二元判别的表现和扩展趋势与模仿学习非常相似。最后，我们研究了一个“偏好模型预训练”阶段，旨在提高在人类偏好上微调时的样本效率。

contrib.en: Shows that prompting improves alignment evaluations with model size, with negligible alignment tax on large models.
contrib.zh: 表明提示随模型规模提升对齐评估效果，且对大型模型几乎没有对齐代价。

contrib.en: Finds ranked preference modeling outperforms imitation learning and scales more favorably with model size.
contrib.zh: 发现排序偏好建模优于模仿学习，且随模型规模扩展更有利。

contrib.en: Introduces preference model pre-training (PMP) to improve sample efficiency when finetuning on human preferences.
contrib.zh: 提出偏好模型预训练（PMP），提升基于人类偏好微调的样本效率。

contrib.en: Demonstrates that binary discrimination performs similarly to imitation learning, unlike ranked preference modeling.
contrib.zh: 证明二元判别与模仿学习表现相似，不同于排序偏好建模。

contrib.en: Shows context distillation can transfer prompt benefits into model weights without performance loss on large models.
contrib.zh: 展示上下文蒸馏可将提示优势转移到模型权重，且不损害大型模型性能。

contrib.en: Provides evidence that PMP with binary data transfers better than ranked data for downstream alignment tasks.
contrib.zh: 提供证据表明，对于下游对齐任务，二元数据的PMP迁移效果优于排序数据。

limits.en: Alignment evaluations are limited to helpfulness, honesty, and harmlessness criteria, which are subjective.
limits.zh: 对齐评估仅限于有用性、诚实性和无害性标准，这些标准具有主观性。

limits.en: Prompting may not scale to more complex alignment challenges beyond simple imitation.
limits.zh: 提示可能无法扩展到超越简单模仿的更复杂对齐挑战。

limits.en: PMP benefits are mainly observed for models larger than 1B parameters; small models show little improvement.
limits.zh: PMP的优势主要在超过10亿参数的模型中观察到；小模型改进甚微。

limits.en: The study does not address alignment of advanced AI systems with superhuman capabilities.
limits.zh: 该研究未涉及具有超人能力的先进AI系统的对齐问题。

limits.en: Human evaluation data may have biases and limited scalability due to annotator expertise and cost.
limits.zh: 人工评估数据可能因标注者专业性和成本而存在偏差和可扩展性有限。

items.en: Given the broad capabilities of large language models, it should be possible to work towards a general-purpose, text-based assistant that is aligned with human values, meaning that it is helpful, honest, and harmless. As an initial foray in this direction we study simple baseline techniques and evaluations, such as prompting. We find that the benefits from modest interventions increase with model size, generalize to a variety of alignment evaluations, and do not compromise the performance of large models. Next we investigate scaling trends for several training objectives relevant to alignment, comparing imitation learning, binary discrimination, and ranked preference modeling. We find that ranked preference modeling performs much better than imitation learning, and often scales more favorably with model size. In contrast, binary discrimination typically performs and scales very similarly to imitation learning. Finally we study a ‘preference model pre-training’ stage of training, with the goal of improving sample efficiency when finetuning on human preferences.
items.zh: 鉴于大型语言模型的广泛能力，应该可以致力于构建一个通用、基于文本的助手，使其与人类价值观对齐，即有用、诚实且无害。作为这一方向的初步探索，我们研究了简单的基线技术和评估方法，例如提示。我们发现，适度干预带来的益处随模型规模增大而增加，能泛化到各种对齐评估中，且不会损害大型模型的性能。接下来，我们研究了与对齐相关的几种训练目标的缩放趋势，比较了模仿学习、二元判别和排序偏好建模。我们发现，排序偏好建模的表现远优于模仿学习，并且通常随模型规模增大而更有利地缩放。相比之下，二元判别在表现和缩放上通常与模仿学习非常相似。最后，我们研究了一个“偏好模型预训练”训练阶段，旨在提高基于人类偏好微调时的样本效率。

items.en: Contemporary AI models can be difficult to understand, predict, and control. These problems can lead to significant harms when AI systems are deployed, and might produce truly devastating results if future systems are even more powerful and more widely used, and interact with each other and the world in presently unforeseeable ways.
items.zh: 当代AI模型可能难以理解、预测和控制。当AI系统部署时，这些问题可能导致重大危害，如果未来的系统更强大、更广泛使用，并以目前无法预见的方式相互交互并与世界交互，可能会产生真正毁灭性的结果。

items.en: This paper shares some nascent work towards one of our primary, ongoing goals, which is to align general-purpose AI systems with human preferences and values. A great deal of ink has been spilled trying to define what it means for AI systems to be aligned, and to guess at how this might go wrong. We will define an AI as “aligned” if it is, in three words, helpful, honest, and harmless or ‘HHH’. Our alignment efforts aim to measure and address this general problem with large language models.
items.zh: 本文分享了一些初步工作，旨在实现我们主要且持续的目标之一：使通用AI系统与人类偏好和价值观对齐。关于AI系统对齐的含义以及可能出错的方式，已有大量讨论。我们将“对齐”的AI定义为三个词：有用、诚实且无害（HHH）。我们的对齐工作旨在衡量并解决大型语言模型中的这一普遍问题。

items.en: Many researchers and organizations share this goal, but few have pursued it directly. Most research efforts associated with alignment either only pertain to very specialized systems, involve testing a specific alignment technique on a sub-problem, or are rather speculative and theoretical. Our view is that if it’s possible to try to address a problem directly, then one needs a good excuse for not doing so. Historically we had such an excuse: general purpose, highly capable AIs were not available for investigation. But given the broad capabilities of large language models, we think it’s time to tackle alignment directly, and that a research program focused on this goal may have the greatest chance for impact. Furthermore:
items.zh: 许多研究人员和组织共享这一目标，但很少有人直接追求它。大多数与对齐相关的研究工作要么只涉及非常专门的系统，要么在子问题上测试特定对齐技术，要么相当推测性和理论性。我们认为，如果有可能直接尝试解决问题，那么就需要有充分的理由不去这样做。历史上我们曾有这样的借口：通用、高能力的AI无法用于研究。但鉴于大型语言模型的广泛能力，我们认为现在是时候直接解决对齐问题了，并且专注于这一目标的研究计划可能产生最大影响。此外：

items.capEn: Figure 1: We show the format of interactions with AI models for A/B testing and human feedback collection. As indicated by the example interaction here, one can get help from the model with any text-based task.
items.capZh: 图1：我们展示了用于A/B测试和人类反馈收集的AI模型交互格式。如这里的示例交互所示，用户可以在任何基于文本的任务上获得模型的帮助。

items.en: A natural language agent can be subjected to a wide variety of inputs, and so it can fail to be helpful, honest, and harmless in myriad ways. We believe it’s valuable to try to see the full picture of where we’ve made progress on alignment, and where we’re currently falling short. This may remain obscure absent efforts to train general aligned agents and allow them to be probed in any way whatsoever. A very broad definition can also facilitate measurement, since it invites the examiner to pose a wide-variety of challenges.
items.zh: 自然语言智能体可能面临各种输入，因此它可能在无数方面无法做到有用、诚实和无害。我们认为，尝试全面了解我们在对齐方面取得的进展以及目前的不足是有价值的。如果没有训练通用对齐智能体并允许以任何方式对其进行探测，这一点可能仍然模糊。非常宽泛的定义也有助于衡量，因为它邀请检查者提出各种挑战。

items.en: By studying a variety of alignment techniques in a general setting, it becomes much easier to compare them and to determine which techniques are simplest and most effective. Some techniques, such as the use of human feedback, are complex and potentially costly, so we’re interested in strategies that can increase their efficiency and focus their application exclusively on goals that cannot be attained more easily in another way.
items.zh: 通过在通用环境中研究各种对齐技术，更容易比较它们并确定哪些技术最简单、最有效。一些技术，如使用人类反馈，复杂且可能成本高昂，因此我们对能够提高其效率并仅将其应用于无法通过其他方式更轻松实现的目标的策略感兴趣。

items.en: Some view alignment as a highly speculative problem, or one that distracts from work on more pressing issues with existing AI systems. In our view, the societal impacts of current AI models should be taken seriously, and the evaluation of current models should be seen as an essential safety project. We believe that training a large language model to be helpful, honest, and harmless (we are not claiming to have achieved this goal!) would represent significant progress towards alleviating the negative societal impacts from general-purpose language models.
items.zh: 有些人将对齐视为一个高度推测性的问题，或者一个分散对现有AI系统更紧迫问题关注的问题。我们认为，当前AI模型的社会影响应被认真对待，对当前模型的评估应被视为一项基本的安全项目。我们相信，训练一个有用、诚实且无害的大型语言模型（我们并未声称已实现这一目标！）将代表在减轻通用语言模型的负面社会影响方面取得重大进展。

items.en: Some of the researchers who are most concerned about the alignment problem believe that aligning extremely capable AIs will be qualitatively different from aligning current more limited systems. We share this concern, but we believe the best vantage point from which to explore alignment for increasingly advanced AIs will be to first establish an aligned baseline at current capability levels. If this were successful, we would then turn to the task of studying progress more deeply, including its scaling properties, and attempt to adversarially validate it. Conversely, if we and others persistently fail, we can identify the thorniest issues with alignment. Halting progress would also provide a persuasive argument for allocating more and more resources towards AI alignment, and for more cautious norms around scaling up and deploying models.
items.zh: 一些最关注对齐问题的研究人员认为，对齐极其强大的AI将与对齐当前更有限的系统有本质不同。我们也有此担忧，但我们认为，探索日益先进的AI对齐的最佳视角是首先在当前能力水平上建立对齐基线。如果成功，我们将转向更深入地研究进展，包括其缩放特性，并尝试对抗性验证。相反，如果我们和其他人持续失败，我们可以识别对齐中最棘手的问题。停滞不前也将提供一个有说服力的论据，用于分配更多资源给AI对齐，并采取更谨慎的规范来扩展和部署模型。

items.en: In pursuit of these goals, in this work we will be investigating the following questions:
items.zh: 为实现这些目标，本文我们将研究以下问题：

items.en: Is naive prompting a workable baseline for alignment? How does it scale, how does it compare to finetuning, and how can we leverage its advantages? We find that prompts induce favorable scaling on a variety of alignment-relevant evaluations, impose negligible ‘taxes’ on large models, and can be ‘context distilled’ back into the original model.
items.zh: 简单的提示是否是可行的对齐基线？它如何缩放，与微调相比如何，以及如何利用其优势？我们发现，提示在各种对齐相关评估中诱导出有利的缩放，对大型模型施加可忽略的“税收”，并且可以“上下文蒸馏”回原始模型。

items.en: When and how much does preference modeling improve on imitation learning? We find that preference modeling improves on and scales more favorably than imitation learning when preferences are part of a ranked hierarchy or continuum (e.g. rank these responses in order of helpfulness), rather than associated with a binary choice (e.g. does this python function pass tests).
items.zh: 偏好建模何时以及在多大程度上优于模仿学习？我们发现，当偏好是排序层次或连续体的一部分（例如，按有用性对这些回答排序）时，偏好建模优于模仿学习且缩放更有利，而不是与二元选择相关（例如，这个Python函数是否通过测试）。

items.en: How can we improve the sample efficiency of preference modeling? We find that we can significantly improve sample efficiency using a ‘preference model pre-training’ (PMP) stage of training, where we first pre-train on large public datasets that encode human preference information, such as Stack Exchange, Reddit, and Wikipedia edits, before finetuning on smaller datasets encoding more specific human preferences.
items.zh: 如何提高偏好建模的样本效率？我们发现，通过“偏好模型预训练”（PMP）训练阶段可以显著提高样本效率，即首先在编码人类偏好信息的大型公共数据集（如Stack Exchange、Reddit和维基百科编辑）上进行预训练，然后在编码更具体人类偏好的较小数据集上进行微调。

items.en: The last two points are particularly important for work using reinforcement learning (RL) for alignment, where the reward signals are predicted by a preference model. In particular, we expect bandit-type RL performance to improve roughly in proportion with preference modeling capabilities, since the preference model’s recognition of high-performance behavior should be closely related to the RL agent’s ability to achieve it. We anticipate that such a strategy can outperform imitation learning on some problems, especially those whose solutions lie on a ranked hierarchy. A similar approach applying human feedback to greatly improve the performance of language models on summary-writing had already been demonstrated [SOW+20].
items.zh: 最后两点对于使用强化学习（RL）进行对齐的工作尤其重要，其中奖励信号由偏好模型预测。特别是，我们预计赌博机式RL的性能将大致与偏好建模能力成比例提高，因为偏好模型对高性能行为的识别应与RL智能体实现它的能力密切相关。我们预计这种策略可以在某些问题上优于模仿学习，尤其是那些解决方案位于排序层次上的问题。类似的方法应用人类反馈来大幅提高语言模型在摘要写作上的性能已经得到证明[SOW+20]。

items.capEn: Figure 2: Left: Simple prompting significantly improves performance and scaling on our HHH alignment evaluations (y-axis measures accuracy at choosing better responses on our HHH evaluations). Right: Prompts impose little or no ‘alignment tax’ on large models, even on complex evaluations like function synthesis. Here we have evaluated our python code models on the HumanEval codex dataset [CTJ+21] at temperature T=0.6𝑇0.6T=0.6 and top P=0.95𝑃0.95P=0.95.
items.capZh: 图2：左图：简单的提示显著提高了我们在HHH对齐评估上的性能和缩放（y轴测量在我们的HHH评估中选择更好回答的准确率）。右图：提示对大型模型施加很少或没有“对齐税”，即使在像函数合成这样的复杂评估上也是如此。这里我们在HumanEval codex数据集[CTJ+21]上评估了我们的Python代码模型，温度为T=0.6，top P=0.95。

items.en: We chose ‘helpful, honest, and harmless’ as criteria because they are simple and memorable, and seem to capture the majority of what we want from an aligned111We also discuss the HHH specification in relation to our broader understanding of alignment in Appendix E. AI. But these are also subtle and ambiguous criteria, and the best AI behavior will involve a compromise between them. For example, there will clearly be conflicts between helpfulness to the user and harmlessness to others if agents are asked to aid in harmful activities. Here are some very brief notes on these terms:
items.zh: 我们选择“有用、诚实且无害”作为标准，因为它们简单易记，并且似乎捕捉了我们从对齐AI中想要的大部分内容。但这些也是微妙且模糊的标准，最佳的AI行为将涉及它们之间的折衷。例如，如果智能体被要求协助有害活动，对用户的有用性和对他人的无害性之间显然会有冲突。以下是对这些术语的一些简要说明：

items.en: The AI should make a clear attempt to perform the task or answer the question posed (as long as this isn’t harmful). It should do this as concisely and efficiently as possible.
items.zh: AI应明确尝试执行任务或回答问题（只要不有害）。它应尽可能简洁高效地完成。

items.en: When more information is required, the AI should ask relevant follow-up questions and obtain necessary details. It should respond with appropriate levels of sensitivity, insight, and discretion.
items.zh: 当需要更多信息时，AI应询问相关的后续问题并获取必要细节。它应以适当的敏感度、洞察力和谨慎度回应。

items.en: Ideally the AI will also re-direct ill-informed requests, e.g. if asked ‘how can I build a website in assembly language’ it might suggest a different approach.
items.zh: 理想情况下，AI还应重新引导信息不足的请求，例如，如果被问“如何用汇编语言建网站”，它可能会建议不同的方法。

items.en: At its most basic level, the AI should give accurate information. Moreover, it should be calibrated (e.g. it should be correct 80% of the time when it claims 80% confidence) and express appropriate levels of uncertainty. It should express its uncertainty without misleading human users.
items.zh: 在最基本的层面上，AI应提供准确的信息。此外，它应经过校准（例如，当声称80%置信度时，应有80%的正确率）并表达适当的不确定性。它应在不误导人类用户的情况下表达其不确定性。

items.en: Crucially, the AI should be honest about its own capabilities and levels of knowledge – it is not sufficient for it to simply imitate the responses expected from a seemingly humble and honest expert.
items.zh: 至关重要的是，AI 应诚实地说明自身能力和知识水平——仅仅模仿一个看似谦逊诚实的专家所期望的回应是不够的。

items.en: Ideally the AI would also be honest about itself and its own internal state, insofar as that information is available to it.
items.zh: 理想情况下，AI 还应就自身及其内部状态（在信息可获取的范围内）保持诚实。

items.en: Honesty is more objective than helpfulness and harmlessness, so more aspects of honesty training may be possible without human input. This might include calibration training on factual claims and claims about the internal state of the model, and the use of search [KSW21] to augment accuracy.
items.zh: 诚实比有益和无害更客观，因此无需人类输入即可进行更多方面的诚实训练。这可能包括对事实性主张和模型内部状态主张的校准训练，以及使用搜索 [KSW21] 来提高准确性。

items.en: The AI should not be offensive or discriminatory, either directly or through subtext or bias.
items.zh: AI 不应具有攻击性或歧视性，无论是直接还是通过潜台词或偏见。

items.en: When asked to aid in a dangerous act (e.g. building a bomb), the AI should politely refuse. Ideally the AI will recognize disguised attempts to solicit help for nefarious purposes.
items.zh: 当被要求协助危险行为（例如制造炸弹）时，AI 应礼貌拒绝。理想情况下，AI 应能识别伪装成寻求帮助的恶意企图。

items.en: To the best of its abilities, the AI should recognize when it may be providing very sensitive or consequential advice and act with appropriate modesty and care.
items.zh: AI 应尽最大努力识别何时可能提供非常敏感或后果严重的建议，并表现出适当的谦逊和谨慎。

items.en: What behaviors are considered harmful and to what degree will vary across people and cultures. It will also be context-dependent, i.e. it will depend on the nature of the user query, who is using the AI assistant, and the time and place in which the assistant is being used.
items.zh: 哪些行为被认为有害以及有害程度如何，会因人和文化而异。这也取决于上下文，即取决于用户查询的性质、使用 AI 助手的用户以及使用助手的时间和地点。

items.en: All of these criteria are at least somewhat subjective, and those who deploy an AI will need to take responsibility for the way that alignment is defined and the extent to which it has been attained.
items.zh: 所有这些标准至少在一定程度上是主观的，部署 AI 的人需要负责定义对齐的方式以及实现对齐的程度。

items.en: We use open-ended natural language dialogue for interaction with our models, with an example pictured in figure 1. We allow for general inputs of essentially arbitrary length from human users, which can include examples, documents, programming code, etc, and we allow similarly general responses from our models. Models indicate they have completed a response by generating a stop sequence, which is literally the string Human: used to designate roles in the dialogue. By default we show two responses and allow users to choose one. We typically request that users pick the most helpful and honest response, as pictured. We use this interface both to A/B test different models and to collect human feedback data. We can use a very similar interface for other safety-related tasks, such as red-teaming the model against harmfulness.
items.zh: 我们使用开放式自然语言对话与模型进行交互，图 1 中展示了一个示例。我们允许人类用户输入任意长度的通用输入，可以包括示例、文档、编程代码等，并且我们允许模型给出同样通用的响应。模型通过生成停止序列来表示已完成响应，该序列实际上是字符串“Human:”，用于指定对话中的角色。默认情况下，我们显示两个响应并允许用户选择一个。我们通常要求用户选择最有帮助和最诚实的响应，如图所示。我们使用此界面进行不同模型的 A/B 测试以及收集人类反馈数据。我们还可以使用非常相似的界面执行其他安全相关任务，例如针对有害性对模型进行红队测试。

items.en: To evaluate performance we created a small dataset of evaluations associated with helpfulness, honesty, harms, and other behaviors in this interactive format. We are sharing these evaluations on BIG Bench for others to try. We also evaluate models and interventions via A/B testing with humans, who have been instructed to solicit models’ help with arbitrary text-based tasks.
items.zh: 为了评估性能，我们创建了一个小型评估数据集，涉及这种交互格式下的有益性、诚实性、有害性及其他行为。我们正在 BIG Bench 上分享这些评估，供其他人尝试。我们还通过 A/B 测试（指示人类用户请求模型帮助完成任意基于文本的任务）来评估模型和干预措施。

items.capEn: Figure 3: In this figure the y-axis measures the accuracy difference of preference modeling compared to imitation learning, where evaluations have been categorized as having either ranked or binary preferences. The light blue curves show ranked evaluations from Learn to Summarize, HellaSwag, and Utilitarianism (ethics); while light orange curves show binary evaluations from Code Correctness, Lambada, Commonsense Morality (ethics), Justice (ethics), Deontology (ethics), and Virtue (ethics). Dark colored curves show the mean over light curves of the same color. All these datasets are evaluated by some form of accuracy, although the specific interpretation is different in each case (e.g., multiple choice accuracy for HellaSwag, pairwise comparison accuracy for Learn to Summarize; see section 3.2). We see that on ranked evaluations, PM performs and scales significantly better than IL (blue), while on binary evaluations there is little discernible difference (orange). The 52B Code Correctness is excluded due to significant compute needed to generate code samples.
items.capZh: 图 3：此图中 y 轴测量偏好建模相对于模仿学习的准确率差异，其中评估已分类为具有排序偏好或二元偏好。浅蓝色曲线显示来自 Learn to Summarize、HellaSwag 和功利主义（伦理学）的排序评估；而浅橙色曲线显示来自代码正确性、Lambada、常识道德（伦理学）、正义（伦理学）、义务论（伦理学）和美德（伦理学）的二元评估。深色曲线显示相同颜色浅曲线的平均值。所有这些数据集都通过某种形式的准确率进行评估，尽管具体解释在每种情况下有所不同（例如，HellaSwag 的多项选择准确率，Learn to Summarize 的成对比较准确率；参见第 3.2 节）。我们看到，在排序评估上，PM 的表现和扩展性显著优于 IL（蓝色），而在二元评估上几乎没有明显差异（橙色）。由于生成代码样本需要大量算力，因此排除了 52B 代码正确性。

items.en: Large language models engage in few-shot learning [BMR+20]. To generically elicit the sort of behavior shown in figure 1, we found that it was sufficient to provide a long prompt (4600 words from 14 fictional conversations) with example interactions. The prompt we used was not carefully designed or optimized for performance on evaluations; rather it was just written by two of us in an ad hoc manner prior to the construction of any evaluations. Despite the fact that our prompt222Prompt text and contractor instructions are at https://gist.github.com/jareddk/2509330f8ef3d787fc5aaac67aab5f11 did not include any examples where models resisted manipulation, refused requests to aid in dangerous activities, or took a stand against unsavory behavior, we observed that models often actively avoided engaging in harmful behaviors based only on the AI ‘personality’ imbued by the prompt. This is reflected in the performance trends on harmfulness in figure 6.
items.zh: 大型语言模型会进行少样本学习 [BMR+20]。为了泛化地引发图 1 所示的行为，我们发现提供包含示例交互的长提示（来自 14 个虚构对话的 4600 个词）就足够了。我们使用的提示并非精心设计或针对评估性能进行优化；它只是由我们中的两人在构建任何评估之前临时编写的。尽管我们的提示222提示文本和承包商说明位于 https://gist.github.com/jareddk/2509330f8ef3d787fc5aaac67aab5f11 不包含任何模型抵制操纵、拒绝协助危险活动或反对不良行为的示例，但我们观察到，仅基于提示赋予的 AI“个性”，模型通常主动避免参与有害行为。这反映在图 6 中有害性的性能趋势中。

items.en: In section 2 we explore the effects of the prompt. In the small data limit, prompting a generative language model may be qualitatively different from and superior to finetuning, since prompting imposes a prior, while finetuning alters the model’s expectations for the underlying data distribution. We make several points concerning prompting:
items.zh: 在第 2 节中，我们探讨了提示的效果。在小数据限制下，提示生成语言模型可能在性质上不同于微调并优于微调，因为提示施加了先验，而微调改变了模型对底层数据分布的期望。我们提出以下几点关于提示的说明：

items.en: We find that prompting can be superior to finetuning in the limit of very small datasets associated with alignment.
items.zh: 我们发现，在与对齐相关的非常小的数据集限制下，提示可以优于微调。

items.en: The prompt context ‘C’ can be distilled into a new language model that models the distribution P​(X|C)𝑃conditional𝑋𝐶P(X|C) instead of P​(X)𝑃𝑋P(X); this is accomplished by simply finetuning with a loss given by the KL divergence between P​(X|C)𝑃conditional𝑋𝐶P(X|C) and the distilled model’s predictions. This procedure has more beneficial effects as compared to finetuning on the prompt.
items.zh: 提示上下文“C”可以蒸馏成一个新的语言模型，该模型对分布 P(X|C) 而非 P(X) 进行建模；这可以通过使用 P(X|C) 与蒸馏模型预测之间的 KL 散度作为损失进行微调来实现。与直接在提示上微调相比，此过程具有更多有益效果。

items.en: The capabilities of small models (e.g. on NLP or coding evaluations) are typically diminished in the presence of the prompt, presumably because they are confused by it. But larger models perform at roughly the same level with or without the prompt.
items.zh: 小模型（例如在 NLP 或编码评估上）的能力通常在提示存在时下降，可能是因为它们被提示混淆了。但较大的模型在有或没有提示的情况下表现大致相同。

items.en: So perhaps prompt-related techniques can carry alignment efforts further than we initially expected.
items.zh: 因此，与提示相关的技术可能比我们最初预期的更能推动对齐工作。

items.en: Nevertheless, we believe that as an approach to alignment, prompt design will have significant limitations. One concern is that prompts may only be capable of teaching the model to imitate some interpolation of the training distribution, and so will not lead the model to exceed the performance demonstrated in the training set. Concretely, we want the model to be honest about itself and its specific capability level rather than presenting an honest-seeming facade in imitation of its training data (e.g. implying that it is able to book a flight). Advanced AI models may also be trained using a mixture of generative modeling, supervised learning, reinforcement learning, and other techniques. Prompt design may not carry over so straightforwardly after generative models are re-purposed for other tasks.
items.zh: 尽管如此，我们认为作为对齐的一种方法，提示设计将具有显著局限性。一个担忧是，提示可能只能教会模型模仿训练分布的某种插值，因此不会导致模型超越训练集中展示的性能。具体来说，我们希望模型对自己及其特定能力水平保持诚实，而不是模仿其训练数据呈现一个看似诚实的表象（例如暗示它能够预订航班）。高级 AI 模型也可能使用生成建模、监督学习、强化学习和其他技术的混合进行训练。在生成模型被重新用于其他任务后，提示设计可能不会如此直接地延续。

items.en: Beyond prompt design, the next simplest technique is imitation learning from expert examples. But the slightly more complex technique of learning distinctions333Note that if such data is not available, there is an option to generate it, since expert examples can be compared with samples from a model – i.e. we can train a GAN-style discriminator. among preferences—not just what to do but also what not to do—may be more promising. We are interested in when this more involved approach improves on imitation learning, and how each scales with model size.
items.zh: 除了提示设计，下一个最简单的技术是从专家示例中进行模仿学习。但稍微复杂一点的技术——学习偏好之间的区别444注意，如果此类数据不可用，可以选择生成它，因为专家示例可以与模型样本进行比较——即我们可以训练一个 GAN 风格的判别器。——不仅包括该做什么，还包括不该做什么，可能更有前景。我们感兴趣的是，这种更复杂的方法何时能改进模仿学习，以及每种方法如何随模型规模扩展。

items.capEn: Figure 4: Performance gain of preference model pre-training on finetuning evaluations, as measured by accuracy difference relative to no PMP. Different colors represent different PMP datasets, including StackExchange, Reddit, Wikipedia, and a ‘Mix’ of all three. Each line represents a combined (mean) result from Learn to Summarize, HellaSwag, and all five Ethics evaluations. Results are shown for the 52B parameter model only, but similar positive results were also seen for the smaller models.
items.capZh: 图 4：偏好模型预训练在微调评估上的性能增益，以相对于无 PMP 的准确率差异衡量。不同颜色代表不同的 PMP 数据集，包括 StackExchange、Reddit、Wikipedia 以及三者的“混合”。每条线代表来自 Learn to Summarize、HellaSwag 和所有五个伦理学评估的组合（平均）结果。仅显示 52B 参数模型的结果，但较小的模型也观察到了类似的正面结果。

items.en: We find that there seems to be a qualitative distinction between two types of tasks:
items.zh: 我们发现两类任务之间似乎存在质的区别：

items.en: Binary Discrimination, where the data has only two possible labels, such as pass/fail or true/false; some examples include determining if python code passes tests, or determining if an action is morally acceptable or unacceptable
items.zh: 二元判别，即数据只有两种可能的标签，例如通过/失败或真/假；一些例子包括判断Python代码是否通过测试，或判断某个行为在道德上是否可接受或不可接受。

items.en: Ranked Preference Modeling among a tall hierarchy of possibilities, with examples including the popularity of a StackExchange answer, or the quality of a paragraph summary. Note that rankings can be learned from pairwise comparisons even though the underlying data has a ranked ordering. Learning from human preferences [CLB+17] and T-REX IRL [BGNN19] learn from ranked data.
items.zh: 在大量可能性的层级结构中进行排序偏好建模，例子包括StackExchange答案的受欢迎程度，或段落摘要的质量。注意，即使底层数据具有排序顺序，也可以从成对比较中学习排序。从人类偏好中学习[CLB+17]和T-REX IRL[BGNN19]从排序数据中学习。

items.en: As shown in the introductory figure 3, we find that preference modeling performs much better and scales somewhat better than imitation learning, but that binary discrimination does not.
items.zh: 如引言中的图3所示，我们发现偏好建模的表现远优于模仿学习，且扩展性更好，但二元判别并非如此。

items.capEn: Figure 5: Transfer performance at 500 and 5k sequence pairs on downstream finetuning evaluations with PMP (on the ‘Mix’ dataset, shown in violet) vs. without PMP (black). Each curve is averaged across finetuning evaluations Learn to Summarize, HellaSwag, and all five Ethics evaluations. We see that PMP significantly improves sample efficiency with large models.
items.capZh: 图5：在PMP（在'Mix'数据集上，以紫色显示）与无PMP（黑色）的下游微调评估中，500和5000个序列对上的迁移性能。每条曲线是微调评估Learn to Summarize、HellaSwag和所有五个Ethics评估的平均值。我们看到PMP显著提高了大模型的样本效率。

items.en: Models that learn to discriminate and rank human preferences play a natural role in alignment research. Such models can be used as filters, and they can also be leveraged more powerfully as preference models for reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) [CLB+17], in order to train aligned policies. Furthermore, some proposals [CSA18, ICA18] for aligning more advanced AIs use different models to train or evaluate each other, so that the effectiveness and reliability of these techniques may ultimately depend on the performance and robustness of preference models.
items.zh: 学习判别和排序人类偏好的模型在对齐研究中发挥着自然作用。这类模型可以用作过滤器，也可以更强大地用作基于人类反馈的强化学习（RLHF）[CLB+17]的偏好模型，以训练对齐的策略。此外，一些对齐更高级AI的提议[CSA18, ICA18]使用不同的模型来训练或评估彼此，因此这些技术的有效性和可靠性最终可能取决于偏好模型的性能和鲁棒性。

items.en: Preference modeling success may be hampered by small datasets, since a natural way to train these models is through human feedback on samples generated from a policy, as in RLHF or human-in-the-loop training, and high-quality human interaction data may be expensive. Thus a significant consideration is whether we can improve the sample efficiency of these models. For this purpose we experiment with preference model pretraining (PMP), so that the full training procedure includes training sequentially on:
items.zh: 偏好建模的成功可能受到小数据集的阻碍，因为训练这些模型的一种自然方式是通过人类对策略生成的样本进行反馈，如RLHF或人在回路训练，而高质量的人类交互数据可能很昂贵。因此，一个重要的考虑因素是我们能否提高这些模型的样本效率。为此，我们尝试了偏好模型预训练（PMP），使得完整的训练过程包括依次训练：

items.en: For the second stage, we utilize large scale public data from Stack Exchange, Reddit, and reverted vandalism444By this we mean that we specifically sourced changes to Wikipedia that were noted as such and quickly reverted. of Wikipedia. We find that this PMP stage of training significantly improves sample efficiency and often improves the asymptotic performance when preference models are finetuned on both human feedback datasets or various alignment-focused datasets.
items.zh: 对于第二阶段，我们利用来自Stack Exchange、Reddit和维基百科的已回退破坏行为的大规模公共数据。我们发现，这种PMP训练阶段显著提高了样本效率，并且当偏好模型在人类反馈数据集或各种对齐重点数据集上进行微调时，通常还能改善渐近性能。

items.en: In appendices we discuss details of model training and dataset preparation and some additional experiments with GAN-style discriminator.
items.zh: 在附录中，我们讨论了模型训练和数据集准备的细节，以及一些使用GAN风格判别器的额外实验。

items.en: Throughout this paper we will be studying a consistent set of decoder-only Transformer language models with parameter counts ranging from about 10M to 52B in increments of 4x, and with a fixed context window of 819281928192 tokens and a 216superscript2162^{16} token vocabulary. For language model pre-training, these models are trained for 400B tokens on a distribution consisting mostly of filtered Common Crawl data [Fou] and internet books, along with a number of smaller distributions [GBB+20], including about 10% python code data. We fix the aspect ratio of our models so that the activation dimension dmodel=128​nlayersubscript𝑑model128subscript𝑛layerd_{\mathrm{model}}=128n_{\mathrm{layer}}, and include models with 13M, 42M, 197M, 810M, 2.7B, 13B, and 52B non-embedding parameters. Throughout the paper we will show results and comparisons as a function of model size, and by ‘Number of Parameters’ we will always mean non-embedding parameters.
items.zh: 在本文中，我们将研究一组一致的仅解码器Transformer语言模型，参数数量从约10M到52B，以4倍递增，固定上下文窗口为8192个token，词汇表大小为2^16个token。对于语言模型预训练，这些模型在主要由过滤后的Common Crawl数据[Fou]和互联网书籍组成的数据分布上训练了400B个token，此外还有一些较小的数据分布[GBB+20]，包括约10%的Python代码数据。我们固定模型的宽高比，使得激活维度d_model = 128 * n_layer，并包括具有13M、42M、197M、810M、2.7B、13B和52B非嵌入参数的模型。在整篇论文中，我们将展示结果和比较作为模型大小的函数，并且“参数数量”始终指非嵌入参数。

items.en: In some places we will also study the properties of these models after they have been finetuned on a pure distribution of python code. We also discuss finetuning on a variety of other datasets, including with additional heads that can make real-valued predictions at all token positions. Most of these finetuning datasets do not utilize the full 8192-token context window, so in many cases we restrict to shorter contexts during finetuning. For a more detailed description of language model pre-training see Appendix A.
items.zh: 在某些地方，我们还将研究这些模型在纯Python代码分布上微调后的性质。我们还讨论了在各种其他数据集上的微调，包括使用可以在所有token位置进行实值预测的额外头部。大多数这些微调数据集并未利用完整的8192个token上下文窗口，因此在许多情况下，我们在微调期间限制为较短的上下文。关于语言模型预训练的详细描述，请参见附录A。

items.en: On prompting, alignment evaluations, alignment taxes, and context distillation:
items.zh: 关于提示、对齐评估、对齐税和上下文蒸馏：

items.en: A simple prompt provides a workable baseline for alignment, and leads to significant improvements on a variety of evaluations (figure 2), including a helpfulness, honesty, and harm evaluation we have written. We introduce ‘context distillation’ and show that it behaves similarly to prompting.
items.zh: 简单的提示为对齐提供了可行的基线，并在各种评估（图2）上带来了显著改进，包括我们编写的帮助性、诚实性和危害性评估。我们引入了“上下文蒸馏”，并表明其行为类似于提示。

items.en: The prompt reduces toxicity [GGS+20] (figure 8) and seemingly leads larger models to be more accurate than smaller models on TruthfulQA [LHE21] (figure 6). Prompted models are significantly preferred by people who interact with them (figure 9).
items.zh: 提示降低了毒性[GGS+20]（图8），并似乎使较大模型在TruthfulQA[LHE21]上比小模型更准确（图6）。与提示模型交互的人明显更偏好它们（图9）。

items.en: Prompting can have negative effects on the capabilities of small models, but has small and sometimes positive effects on large models, which therefore pay little ‘alignment tax’ (figure 2).
items.zh: 提示可能对小模型的能力产生负面影响，但对大模型影响较小，有时甚至产生正面影响，因此它们支付的“对齐税”很小（图2）。

items.en: On the comparative scaling of imitation learning, binary discrimination, and preference modeling:
items.zh: 关于模仿学习、二元判别和偏好建模的比较扩展：

items.en: The scaling of binary discrimination does not improve very significantly on the scaling of imitation learning (see figure 3 for a summary, and figure 12 for detailed results on Code Correctness).
items.zh: 二元判别的扩展性并没有显著优于模仿学习（参见图3的总结，以及图12中关于代码正确性的详细结果）。

items.en: Ranked preference modeling of complex hierarchies greatly improves on imitation learning. This should be encouraging news for alignment work based on human preferences.
items.zh: 复杂层级结构的排序偏好建模大大优于模仿学习。这对于基于人类偏好的对齐工作来说应该是一个令人鼓舞的消息。

items.en: These conclusions hold rather cleanly and consistently as represented by at least three distinct datasets in each category (see figures 3, 14, and 15), but we would still suggest that further work may improve our understanding of these findings.
items.zh: 这些结论在至少三个不同数据集上（见图3、14和15）表现得相当清晰和一致，但我们仍然建议进一步的工作可能会改善我们对这些发现的理解。

items.en: On preference modeling pre-training (PMP) for improved sample efficiency:
items.zh: 关于偏好模型预训练（PMP）以提高样本效率：

items.en: A PMP stage of training between basic language model pretraining and finetuning on small final datasets significantly improves sample efficiency (see figures 4 and 5 for summaries, and figure 17 for details).
items.zh: 在基础语言模型预训练与小型最终数据集微调之间加入PMP训练阶段，能显著提升样本效率（摘要见图4和图5，详情见图17）。

items.en: These results hold even when the PMP data are quite different from the final dataset (e.g. finetuning from Stack Exchange to summarization).
items.zh: 即使PMP数据与最终数据集差异很大（例如从Stack Exchange微调到摘要生成），这些结果依然成立。

items.en: In marked contrast to the scaling results mentioned earlier, where PM scales best on hierarchically ranked datasets, we find that it’s better for the PMP stage of training to focus on binary discrimination (see figure 18). An explanation for the better performance of binary PMP may be that hierarchies of preferences are difficult to quickly unlearn during finetuning, whereas binary discrimination training teaches models the correct features without establishing strong model preferences. We test this explanation with a quick synthetic data experiment shown in figure 33.
items.zh: 与前述缩放结果（PM在分层排名数据集上表现最佳）形成鲜明对比的是，我们发现PMP训练阶段应侧重于二元判别（见图18）。二元PMP性能更优的解释可能是：偏好层级在微调过程中难以快速遗忘，而二元判别训练则教会模型正确的特征，同时不建立强烈的模型偏好。我们通过图33所示的快速合成数据实验验证了这一解释。

items.en: We also try training the preference model to discriminate between human- and model-generated samples for the PMP step, and find that it also performs well, as shown in figure 19.
items.zh: 我们还尝试在PMP步骤中训练偏好模型区分人类生成与模型生成的样本，发现其同样表现良好，如图19所示。

full.secZh: 2 基于对齐行为的条件化

items.capEn: Figure 6: Left: We show the HHH evaluation performance broken down by category. The improvements on the Harm evaluations suggest a form of generalization, as the prompt does not contain any examples where the assistant resists engaging in harmful behavior. Right: We show results on the adversarial TruthfulQA dataset (MC1), which was constructed so that larger models would perform more poorly. The context-distilled prompt seems to improve the performance of the largest models. The solid lines correspond to the official evaluation using total probability for each response; we also show the mutual information metric for comparison.
items.capZh: 图6：左图：按类别展示HHH评估性能。Harm评估的改进表明存在某种泛化形式，因为提示中不包含任何助手拒绝参与有害行为的示例。右图：展示对抗性TruthfulQA数据集（MC1）的结果，该数据集设计使得较大模型表现更差。上下文蒸馏提示似乎提升了最大模型的性能。实线对应使用每个响应总概率的官方评估；同时展示互信息指标以作比较。

items.en: Large language models can be guided towards desirable behaviors by taking advantage of their in-context learning abilities. Given a suitable prompt, models will take on the style and persona implicit in the prompt and continue to behave mostly in the same vein. This technique can leverage small quantities of very high quality data, and it has the advantage that the prompt can be easily interpreted by humans. For a variety of reasons we do not expect that prompting will produce fully aligned behavior, but it provides a very useful baseline.
items.zh: 大型语言模型可通过利用其上下文学习能力被引导至期望行为。给定合适的提示，模型会采纳提示中隐含的风格和角色，并继续以类似方式行事。该技术可利用少量高质量数据，且优势在于提示易于人类理解。出于多种原因，我们预计提示不会产生完全对齐的行为，但它提供了一个非常有用的基线。

items.en: In this section we will study a variety of zero-shot evaluations for alignment with and without prompting. The prompt we use consists of fourteen human-assistant conversations, where the assistant is always polite, helpful, and accurate. The prompt does not contain examples where the assistant actively resists aiding in harmful behavior, but nevertheless for simplicity we will refer to it as the ‘HHH prompt’ or simply the prompt in what follows. We find that although the effect of prompting is modest when measured against the overall goal of alignment, it improves alignment (according to our evaluations) and decreases toxicity. A potentially more important observation is that the prompt improves trends, so that alignment improves with model size, including on TruthfulQA [LHE21], a dataset designed specifically to induce the opposite trend. Furthermore, we show that there is little ‘tax’ from alignment – at large model size capabilities are not significantly impaired by the prompt. Of course, this does not mean that more intensive alignment interventions will incur no cost.
items.zh: 本节将研究多种零样本对齐评估，包括有提示和无提示的情况。我们使用的提示包含十四段人机对话，其中助手始终礼貌、乐于助人且准确。提示中不包含助手主动拒绝参与有害行为的示例，但为简便起见，下文将其称为“HHH提示”或简称提示。我们发现，尽管从对齐的总体目标衡量，提示的效果适中，但它改善了对齐（根据我们的评估）并降低了毒性。一个可能更重要的观察是，提示改善了趋势，使得对齐随模型规模提升而改善，包括在TruthfulQA [LHE21]上——该数据集专门设计用于诱导相反趋势。此外，我们表明对齐的“代价”很小——在大模型规模下，提示并未显著损害能力。当然，这并不意味着更密集的对齐干预不会产生成本。

items.en: We also introduce a ‘context distillation’ technique that may make prompting more efficient in practice and potentially allow for the use of prompts that exceed the size of the context window. For many but not all of our evaluations context distillation performs about as well as prompting. We begin by briefly describing this method, and then we will discuss evaluations.
items.zh: 我们还引入了一种“上下文蒸馏”技术，该技术可能使提示在实践中更高效，并可能允许使用超出上下文窗口大小的提示。在我们的许多（但非全部）评估中，上下文蒸馏的表现与提示相当。我们首先简要描述该方法，然后讨论评估。

items.en: Sampling from a language model with a prepended prompt has several disadvantages: the prompt occupies useful space in a finite context window, which also limits the total prompt length, and without special affordances the prompt will waste compute and memory when sampling.
items.zh: 从带有前置提示的语言模型中采样存在若干缺点：提示占用有限上下文窗口中的有用空间，从而限制了总提示长度，且在没有特殊处理的情况下，提示会在采样时浪费算力和内存。

items.en: One way to avoid all of these problems is to finetune on the prompt. This invites some practical difficulties, since we need to finetune on a tiny dataset without limiting model capabilities. But finetuning also behaves differently from prompting – finetuning changes the model’s expectations for the data distribution P​(X)𝑃𝑋P(X), bringing it closer to the distribution of the prompt P​(C)𝑃𝐶P(C), whereas prompting instead asks the model for the distribution P​(X|C)𝑃conditional𝑋𝐶P(X|C), where C𝐶C is the context. To give a stark illustration, if we show a language model the list C=1,2,⋯,63𝐶12⋯63C=1,2,\cdots,63 then it will assign very high probability that the numbers X=64,65,⋯𝑋6465⋯X=64,65,\cdots are coming next. If instead we finetune on C𝐶C, the resulting model will not expect to immediately see the token 646464, though it will catch on to the counting pattern if we continue the sequence. We illustrate this toy experiment in figure 26, which we have relegated to the appendix.
items.zh: 避免所有这些问题的办法之一是在提示上进行微调。这带来一些实际困难，因为我们需要在极小数据集上微调而不限制模型能力。但微调的行为也与提示不同——微调改变模型对数据分布P(X)的期望，使其更接近提示分布P(C)，而提示则要求模型给出分布P(X|C)，其中C是上下文。为直观说明，如果我们向语言模型展示列表C=1,2,...,63，则模型会赋予后续数字X=64,65,...极高的概率。如果我们在C上微调，得到的模型不会期望立即看到token 64，尽管如果继续序列，它会逐渐掌握计数模式。我们在图26中展示了这一玩具实验，该图已移至附录。

items.en: We can both avoid overfitting and take advantage of conditioning via ‘context distillation’, where we finetune a model pθ​(X)subscript𝑝𝜃𝑋p_{\theta}(X) with a loss given by
items.zh: 我们可以通过“上下文蒸馏”同时避免过拟合并利用条件作用，其中我们使用以下损失对模型pθ(X)进行微调：

items.en: where p0subscript𝑝0p_{0} is the initial model, the context C𝐶C is fixed, and the data X𝑋X is drawn from a large corpus of text, such as the original pre-training distribution. We discuss the details of context distillation training in appendix B.5.
items.zh: 其中p0是初始模型，上下文C固定，数据X来自大型文本语料库（如原始预训练分布）。我们在附录B.5中讨论了上下文蒸馏训练的细节。

items.capEn: Figure 7: We show zero-shot Lambada performance in the presence of the HHH prompt and with context distillation. In both cases there is a small ‘alignment tax’.
items.capZh: 图7：展示在HHH提示和上下文蒸馏下零样本Lambada性能。两种情况下都存在较小的“对齐代价”。

items.en: We see from figure 2 that this technique appears to work quite well. However, the benefits compared to simply finetuning on the prompt become much less significant if we additionally provide a small prompt after the finetuning or distillation process, as shown in figure 20 in the appendix. It appears that contractors interacting with our models observe a small degradation from distillation, as seen in figure 9. In the future it might be interesting to apply context distillation iteratively, which one might liken to loading the model with a long-term memory or pseudo-identity.
items.zh: 从图2可以看出，该技术似乎效果不错。然而，与仅在提示上微调相比，如果在微调或蒸馏过程后额外提供一个小提示，其优势会显著降低，如附录图20所示。与我们的模型交互的承包商观察到蒸馏带来的轻微性能下降，如图9所示。未来，迭代应用上下文蒸馏可能很有趣，这可以类比为向模型加载长期记忆或伪身份。

items.en: As a first step in evaluating our models, the authors wrote about fifty comparison evaluations for each category of helpfulness, honesty,555Our evaluations of ‘honesty’ are probably the most correlated with model capabilities, as they measure a mixture of accuracy, preference for expressions of humility, recognition of when another source might be more useful than a language model, and unwillingness to provide inaccurate information. Whether an AI’s response is honest depends on the expertise of the AI, and a major weakness of our evaluations is that they do not account for this. harmlessness (HHH), and an ‘other’ label, for a total of around two-hundred comparisons, which will be available shortly at BIG Bench. We did not put effort into separating alignment from capabilities, and so even without any alignment-related prompting, we find that larger models do somewhat better overall. In many cases we initially produced several slightly different queries (largely differing by paraphrase) for each comparison, but found that large models were rarely confused by these variations, so for simplicity we dropped them. Results on these evaluations are pictured in figure 2. We expect that more sophisticated alignment techniques should be able to significantly improve these results.
items.zh: 作为评估模型的第一步，作者为帮助性、诚实性、无害性（HHH）和“其他”标签各编写了约五十个比较评估，总共约两百个比较，这些评估将很快在BIG Bench上提供。我们没有努力将对齐与能力分开，因此即使没有对齐相关的提示，我们也发现较大模型总体上表现更好。在许多情况下，我们最初为每个比较生成了几个略有不同的查询（主要差异在于改写），但发现大模型很少被这些变体混淆，因此为简便起见我们删除了它们。这些评估的结果如图2所示。我们预计更复杂的对齐技术应能显著改善这些结果。

items.en: Note that we evaluate model choices using the empirical mutual information I​(a,q)=log⁡[P​(a|q)/P​(a)]𝐼𝑎𝑞𝑃conditional𝑎𝑞𝑃𝑎I(a,q)=\log\left[P(a|q)/P(a)\right] for queries q𝑞q and responses a𝑎a, rather than the more typical choice of mean token probability for the response (mutual information was also used for several evaluations of GPT-3 [BMR+20]). The mutual information metric tends to be useful when responses differ greatly in length, and it makes a significant difference in performance on our evaluations.
items.zh: 注意，我们使用经验互信息I(a,q)=log[P(a|q)/P(a)]（对于查询q和响应a）来评估模型选择，而不是更常用的响应平均token概率（互信息也用于GPT-3的若干评估[BMR+20]）。互信息指标在响应长度差异较大时往往有用，并且对我们的评估性能有显著影响。

items.en: On the left in figure 6 we show the results on our HHH evaluations by category. We found it a bit ironic that the models perform best in the ‘honesty’ category, as the models certainly do fabricate information when probed interactively as general-purpose assistants. To further evaluate our models’ honesty, we include evaluations on TruthfulQA666We wrote the prompt before TruthfulQA was available. That said, we found in other experiments that using TruthfulQA examples as a prompt significantly improves performance (much more than our prompt). This suggests that the phenomenon uncovered by TruthfulQA is not a difficult alignment challenge on its own. MC1 on the right of this figure. We see that the context distilled prompt has slightly improved the performance of our largest models using the standard evaluation777In an earlier version of this paper we mistakenly used a very non-standard formulation of the task. We thank the authors of [LHE21] for pointing out this error, which has been corrected. metric. We also compare the use of more evaluation metrics on TruthfulQA in figure 21 in the appendix. The use of conditional probabilities does not alter trends significantly, but does greatly affect absolute performance.
items.zh: 在图6左侧，我们按类别展示了HHH评估的结果。我们发现模型在“诚实”类别中表现最佳，这有点讽刺，因为当作为通用助手进行交互式探测时，模型确实会编造信息。为进一步评估模型的诚实性，我们在TruthfulQA上进行了评估。我们在该图右侧展示了MC1结果。我们看到，使用标准评估指标，上下文蒸馏提示略微提升了最大模型的性能。我们还在附录图21中比较了TruthfulQA上更多评估指标的使用。条件概率的使用并未显著改变趋势，但极大地影响了绝对性能。

items.capEn: Figure 8: Left: Average toxicity in response to a random sample of 500 prompts labeled as ‘non-toxic’ from the RealToxicityPrompts dataset for language models (LM, blue), prompted language models (LM+Prompt, orange), and context distilled language models (LM+Context Distillation, green). Right: Same as Left, except for a random sample of 500 prompts labeled as Toxic. For non-toxic and toxic prompts, both prompting and context-distillation decrease toxicity and perform similarly to each other as models increase in size. It appears that the prompt leads to decreasing toxicity as model size increases.
items.capZh: 图8：左图：针对RealToxicityPrompts数据集中标记为“非毒性”的500个随机提示，语言模型（LM，蓝色）、提示语言模型（LM+Prompt，橙色）和上下文蒸馏语言模型（LM+Context Distillation，绿色）生成文本的平均毒性。右图：与左图相同，但针对标记为毒性的500个随机提示。对于非毒性和毒性提示，提示和上下文蒸馏均降低了毒性，并且随着模型规模增大，两者表现相似。提示似乎导致毒性随模型规模增大而降低。

items.en: It is noteworthy that larger models tend to perform better on our evaluations in the presence of the HHH prompt, even on categories such as harmlessness that are not directly demonstrated by the prompt. We find this mildly encouraging but unsurprising, since all prior work suggests that larger models have stronger in-context learning capabilities, so that they can more efficiently recognize the implicit framing from the prompt.
items.zh: 值得注意的是，在HHH提示下，较大模型在我们的评估中往往表现更好，即使在提示未直接展示的类别（如无害性）上也是如此。我们认为这略微令人鼓舞但并不意外，因为所有先前工作表明较大模型具有更强的上下文学习能力，因此能更有效地识别提示中的隐含框架。

items.en: We measured the effect of prompting and context distillation on the toxicity of text generated from language models of increasing size. We found that these simple alignment interventions tend to both decrease toxicity and perform similarly to one another (Figure 8). To measure toxicity, we first sampled text conditioned on a random sample of 111K prompts from the RealToxicityPrompts dataset [GGS+20]. The prompts are labeled as either ’toxic’ or ’non-toxic’ and we sample an equal proportion of these prompts. Next, we computed a toxicity score from model samples of text, conditioned on the prompts, using an open source automated toxicity detector [HU20]. Our analysis is similar to to [GGS+20] with a few minor modifications. We provide full details and further analyses in Appendix B.2.
items.zh: 我们测量了提示和上下文蒸馏对不同规模语言模型生成文本毒性的影响。我们发现这些简单的对齐干预措施均倾向于降低毒性，且两者表现相似（图8）。为测量毒性，我们首先基于RealToxicityPrompts数据集[GGS+20]中111K个随机提示的样本生成文本。提示被标记为“毒性”或“非毒性”，我们采样等比例的提示。接着，我们使用开源自动毒性检测器[HU20]计算基于提示的模型文本样本的毒性分数。我们的分析与[GGS+20]类似，但有一些微小修改。我们在附录B.2中提供完整细节和进一步分析。

items.capEn: Figure 9: This figure illustrates the approximate Elo score of various models, fit from the frequency with which contractors viewed a given model as more helpful and honest in head-to-head tests involving pairs of models. Models with the full HHH prompt seem to be slightly preferred over those with a shorter prompt or context distillation. We include 1​σ1𝜎1\sigma error bars for the special cases, which were only compared against the HHH-prompted models of equal size.
items.capZh: 图9：该图展示了各种模型的近似Elo评分，该评分根据承包商在成对模型测试中认为某个模型更有帮助和更诚实的频率拟合得出。使用完整HHH提示的模型似乎比使用较短提示或上下文蒸馏的模型略受青睐。我们为特殊情况包含了1σ误差棒，这些情况仅与同等规模的HHH提示模型进行了比较。

items.en: While these results are encouraging, automated toxicity detection has several known issues [GGS+20, WGU+21]. For example, there can be low agreement in human annotations of toxicity and biases in toxicity labels for certain minorities. We also note that other interventions explicitly designed to reduce toxicity (e.g., fine-tuning models on non-toxic training data, steering/filtering model outputs away from toxic outputs at test time, filtering toxic training data at train time) can yield much larger decreases in automated toxicity scores than the ones we observe here [GGS+20, WGU+21]. Nevertheless, we believe that prompting and context distillation provide a useful baseline for testing the impact of alignment interventions on automated toxicity scores.
items.zh: 尽管这些结果令人鼓舞，但自动毒性检测存在几个已知问题[GGS+20, WGU+21]。例如，人类对毒性的标注一致性可能较低，并且某些少数群体的毒性标签存在偏差。我们还注意到，其他专门设计用于降低毒性的干预措施（例如，在非毒性训练数据上微调模型、在测试时引导/过滤模型输出远离毒性输出、在训练时过滤毒性训练数据）可以比我们在此观察到的结果产生更大的自动毒性评分下降[GGS+20, WGU+21]。尽管如此，我们相信提示和上下文蒸馏为测试对齐干预对自动毒性评分的影响提供了一个有用的基线。

items.en: Using the dialogue interface in figure 1, we evaluated relative model performance via a number of head-to-head tests between pairs of models. This worked as follows. For any given conversation, we would choose a pair of models, with each model writing a single response to each human query. We randomized whether a given model’s responses would appear in position "A" or "B" in the interface, to avoid the possibility that users would consistently find "A" or "B" to be better. We also pegged streaming sampling speed to that of the slowest model, to partially obscure model identity and avoid bias. We collected a total of about 6k individual pair-wise888Note that we typically obtain roughly 3-5 comparisons per conversation. There may be some subtle biases here where weaker models perform more poorly early on in conversations, affecting the possibilities for later dialogue. model comparisons
items.zh: 使用图1中的对话界面，我们通过多组成对模型之间的头对头测试评估了相对模型性能。具体操作如下：对于任何给定的对话，我们选择一对模型，每个模型对每个人类查询写一个响应。我们随机化给定模型的响应出现在界面中“A”或“B”位置，以避免用户一致认为“A”或“B”更好。我们还将流式采样速度固定为最慢模型的速度，以部分掩盖模型身份并避免偏差。我们总共收集了约6000个独立的成对模型比较。

items.en: From this process we collected a table of ‘win rates’ for pairs of models, which we provide in table 2 in the appendix. Here we included fully HHH-prompted models with 200M, 800M, 3B, 13B, and 52B parameters, though we collected somewhat more comparisons involving larger, better-performing models. We also compared the fully prompted 13B and 52B models to their context-distilled versions and to a version with a shorter prompt consisting of only a single999We did not use completely unprompted models because they would be very unlikely to keep to the format of the dialogue or emit appropriate stop sequences. example conversation.
items.zh: 通过这个过程，我们收集了一个模型对之间的“胜率”表，见附录中的表2。这里我们包含了完全HHH提示的200M、800M、3B、13B和52B参数模型，尽管我们收集了更多涉及更大、性能更好模型的比较。我们还将完全提示的13B和52B模型与其上下文蒸馏版本以及仅包含单个示例对话的较短提示版本进行了比较。

items.en: We used these results to estimate a single relative Elo score for each model. Intuitively, this score is similar to that used for ranking Chess players, with a real scalar value based on the relative win rates amongst all players. Quantitatively, we fit the Elo scores from the data in table 2 with the same loss function we use for preference modeling (equation 3.1). We display the results in figure 9, where we recall that a difference of 100 points in an Elo score signifies a ‘win rate’ of 64%percent6464\%.
items.zh: 我们使用这些结果为每个模型估计了一个单一的相对Elo评分。直观上，该评分类似于用于国际象棋棋手排名的评分，基于所有玩家之间的相对胜率得出一个实标量值。定量上，我们使用与偏好建模相同的损失函数（公式3.1）从表2的数据中拟合Elo评分。结果展示在图9中，我们回忆一下，Elo评分相差100分意味着“胜率”为64%。

items.en: The most striking feature of these results is that Elo score appears to be linear in the logarithm of model size from 197M to 13B parameters, but it does not change very significantly between 13B and 52B parameters. We do not believe that this is because the two largest models are equally capable. Rather, we interpret it as a limitation of the training and incentives of the contractors evaluating the models, who are US-based master-qualified MTurkers who were only provided with some simple instructions, and who have an implicit incentive to finish tasks quickly. This provides a sense for how well-trained and capable workers need to be to perceive distinctions among large language models.
items.zh: 这些结果最显著的特点是，从197M到13B参数，Elo评分似乎与模型大小的对数呈线性关系，但在13B到52B参数之间变化不大。我们不认为这是因为两个最大的模型能力相当。相反，我们将其解释为评估模型的承包商的训练和激励的局限性，这些承包商是美国本土的、具备硕士资格的MTurk工作者，他们只得到了一些简单的指示，并且有隐含的激励去快速完成任务。这让我们了解到，需要训练有素且能力强的工人才能感知大型语言模型之间的区别。

items.en: We note that using a much shorter prompt with just one example conversation seems to hurt performance, and it seems that the contractors were able to differentiate the prompted and context distilled model, with the former being preferred about 53% of the time. We include 1-σ𝜎\sigma error bars for these comparisons (note that the short-prompt and distilled models were only compared to the fully prompted models of equal size), so we have some weak evidence that context distillation has degraded performance somewhat compared to the full HHH prompt.
items.zh: 我们注意到，使用仅包含一个示例对话的较短提示似乎会损害性能，并且承包商似乎能够区分提示模型和上下文蒸馏模型，前者被偏好的概率约为53%。我们为这些比较包含了1σ误差棒（注意，短提示和蒸馏模型仅与同等规模的完全提示模型进行了比较），因此我们有弱证据表明上下文蒸馏相对于完整HHH提示在某种程度上降低了性能。

items.capEn: Figure 10: This figure shows performance of our code-finetuned models on the Codex and QuixBugs evaluations with and without the alignment prompt. We see that in both cases, the prompt confuses smaller models, leading to worse performance, but it actively improves the 13B and 52B models. All samples were generated at temperature T=0.6𝑇0.6T=0.6 and top P=0.95𝑃0.95P=0.95 (these settings were not optimized and are not optimal for Pass@1). Note the figure on the left here was also presented in the introduction.
items.capZh: 图10：该图展示了我们的代码微调模型在Codex和QuixBugs评估中，在有和没有对齐提示的情况下的性能。我们看到，在这两种情况下，提示会混淆较小的模型，导致性能下降，但主动提升了13B和52B模型。所有样本均在温度T=0.6和top P=0.95下生成（这些设置未优化，并非Pass@1的最优设置）。注意，左侧的图也在引言中展示过。

items.en: A general concern about alignment is that it may impose a ‘tax’ on performance, such that aligned models may be weaker than raw or unaligned models. In the case of prompting and context distillation, it is straightforward to evaluate this question directly by performing evaluations with and without the prompt. When we include the HHH prompt, we also use the human-assistant framing when presenting the problem or evaluation to the model. The precise specifications can be found in appendix B.1.
items.zh: 关于对齐的一个普遍担忧是，它可能会对性能施加“税”，使得对齐模型可能比原始或未对齐模型更弱。在提示和上下文蒸馏的情况下，直接通过在有和没有提示的情况下进行评估来评估这个问题是直接的。当我们包含HHH提示时，我们在向模型呈现问题或评估时也使用人类-助手框架。具体规格见附录B.1。

items.en: We display results for two very similar python coding evaluations, the Codex HumanEval [CTJ+21] and the QuixBugs challenge reformulated as a function synthesis task [LKCSL17] in figure 10. Interestingly, smaller models perform significantly worse with the prompt, but 13B and 52B models actually perform noticeably better. These evaluations were run using our code-finetuned models, so the strong performance of the larger models also suggests that these models have not lost their ability to process the natural language in the prompt.
items.zh: 我们在图10中展示了两个非常相似的Python代码评估的结果：Codex HumanEval [CTJ+21]和QuixBugs挑战（重新表述为函数合成任务）[LKCSL17]。有趣的是，较小的模型在提示下表现显著更差，但13B和52B模型实际上表现明显更好。这些评估使用我们的代码微调模型运行，因此较大模型的强劲表现也表明这些模型没有失去处理提示中自然语言的能力。

items.en: We performed a similar evaluation on Lambada [PKL+16], with results shown in figure 7. We see that the prompt and context distillation impose a small ‘tax’ on performance that does not have a significant model-size dependence. As shown in Appendix B.4, Lambada performance is strongly dependent on some formatting issues, which alter performance by a much larger margin than the prompt. This format-dependence itself might be regarded as an alignment problem, but unfortunately we do not find that the HHH prompt reduces the difference between accuracies obtained from different Lambada formats.
items.zh: 我们在Lambada [PKL+16]上进行了类似的评估，结果如图7所示。我们看到提示和上下文蒸馏对性能施加了小的“税”，且没有显著的模型大小依赖性。如附录B.4所示，Lambada性能强烈依赖于某些格式问题，这些问题对性能的影响远大于提示。这种格式依赖性本身可能被视为一个对齐问题，但不幸的是，我们发现HHH提示并未减少不同Lambada格式之间准确率的差异。

items.en: We therefore found that while smaller models may be confused by the prompt, larger models’ performance is not heavily impacted by it.
items.zh: 因此，我们发现，虽然较小的模型可能被提示混淆，但较大模型的性能并未受到提示的严重影响。

full.sec: 3 Scaling of Preference Modeling vs Imitation Learning
full.secZh: 3 偏好建模与模仿学习的规模效应

items.en: Alignment requires distinguishing between ‘good’ and ‘bad’ behavior. There are several different training objectives that may be used to accomplish this:
items.zh: 对齐需要区分“好”和“坏”的行为。有几种不同的训练目标可用于实现这一点：

items.en: Imitation Learning: Here we simply train language models to imitate ‘good’ behavior via supervised learning with the usual cross-entropy loss.
items.zh: 模仿学习：这里我们简单地通过使用通常的交叉熵损失进行监督学习来训练语言模型模仿“好”的行为。

items.en: Binary Discrimination: Given a sample of ‘correct’ behavior and a sample of ‘incorrect’ behavior, train the model to distinguish between the two.
items.zh: 二元判别：给定一个“正确”行为的样本和一个“不正确”行为的样本，训练模型区分两者。

items.en: Ranked Preference Modeling: Given a dataset of samples whose overall ‘quality’ is ranked in some way, we train models to output a scalar quality score101010These values could then be used as reward signals for reinforcement learning. for each sample whose value matches the ranking as closely as possible. For simplicity we focus on using pairs of ranked samples (i.e., binary comparisons), and we train our models to assign a higher score to the ‘better’ sample in each pair. In some respects this generalizes binary discrimination, and for uniformity we will use it as the training objective even for binary discrimination tasks (see section 3.1 for details).
items.zh: 排序偏好建模：给定一个数据集，其中样本的总体“质量”以某种方式排序，我们训练模型为每个样本输出一个标量质量分数，该分数的值与排序尽可能匹配。为简单起见，我们专注于使用成对的排序样本（即二元比较），并训练我们的模型为每对中的“更好”样本分配更高的分数。在某些方面，这推广了二元判别，为了统一，我们将使用它作为训练目标，即使是二元判别任务（详见第3.1节）。

items.en: We would like to explore a very general question: when and by how much do discriminators and preference models outperform imitation learning?
items.zh: 我们想探讨一个非常普遍的问题：判别器和偏好模型何时以及多大程度上优于模仿学习？

items.en: Our experiments in this section involve comparing the performance of imitation learning vs. preference modeling on a variety of finetuning evaluations, some of which are binary in nature while others are ranked.
items.zh: 本节中的实验涉及在多种微调评估上比较模仿学习与偏好建模的性能，其中一些是二元的，而另一些是排序的。

items.en: Binary: Code Correctness, Commonsense (ethics), Justice (ethics), Deontology (ethics), Virtue (ethics), Lambada
items.zh: 二元：代码正确性、常识（伦理）、正义（伦理）、义务论（伦理）、美德（伦理）、Lambada

items.en: Ranked: Learn to Summarize, Utility (ethics), HellaSwag
items.zh: 排序：学习摘要、效用（伦理）、HellaSwag

items.en: We focus mostly on alignment-relevant tasks, but include one binary and one ranked NLP task (Lambada [PKL+16] and HellaSwag [ZHB+19], respectively). Code Correctness is a dataset we constructed from python functions in public github repos with test coverage, with correctness determined by unit tests. The Ethics [HBB+21] evaluations are mostly binary classification problems, and so naturally belong in our binary category, except for Utilitarianism which compares relative ‘pleasantness’ of scenarios. The distinction between ranked and binary tasks can be ambiguous—for example, whether code passes tests is binary, but code quality seems like a continuum.
items.zh: 我们主要关注与对齐相关的任务，但包含一个二分类和一个排序的自然语言处理任务（分别是Lambada [PKL+16]和HellaSwag [ZHB+19]）。代码正确性是我们从具有测试覆盖率的公共GitHub仓库中的Python函数构建的数据集，正确性由单元测试确定。伦理评估 [HBB+21] 大多是二分类问题，因此自然属于我们的二分类类别，但功利主义除外，它比较场景的相对“愉悦度”。排序任务和二分类任务之间的区别可能模糊——例如，代码是否通过测试是二元的，但代码质量似乎是一个连续体。

items.en: Our results support a simple conclusion summarized in figure 3: Ranked preference models tend to improve greatly on imitation learning, but binary discrimination typically provides little benefit.
items.zh: 我们的结果支持一个简单的结论，总结在图3中：排序偏好模型通常在模仿学习上有很大提升，但二分类判别通常收益甚微。

items.en: In some respects this conclusion is quite intuitive: to apply imitation learning to preference modeling, one must either only train on the very best data (limiting the dataset size) or train to imitate a lot of examples of lower quality. Nonetheless, the magnitude of the gains are rather stark.
items.zh: 在某些方面，这个结论相当直观：要将模仿学习应用于偏好建模，要么只能训练最好的数据（限制数据集大小），要么训练模仿大量低质量样本。尽管如此，提升的幅度相当显著。

items.en: In many cases it is also possible to study the robustness of various methods for ranking samples. For example, if we sample many responses to a prompt/query, we would like to know if the highest ranked samples according to a given preference model are truly the best. We test this behavior directly in our code correctness studies and with Lambada.
items.zh: 在许多情况下，还可以研究各种排序样本方法的鲁棒性。例如，如果我们对一个提示/查询采样多个响应，我们想知道根据给定偏好模型排名最高的样本是否真的是最好的。我们在代码正确性研究和Lambada中直接测试了这种行为。

full.sec: 3.1 Loss and Settings for Preference Modeling and Imitation Learning
full.secZh: 3.1 偏好建模与模仿学习的损失与设置

items.en: Our preference models consist of a value head that predicts a single scalar ‘score’ r𝑟r on top of the final token of any given context, with larger r𝑟r indicating more desirable samples. The preference modeling loss for each pair of ‘good’ and ‘bad’ sequences is [CLB+17]
items.zh: 我们的偏好模型由一个价值头组成，该价值头在任何给定上下文的最后一个token上预测一个标量“分数” r，较大的 r 表示更理想的样本。每对“好”和“坏”序列的偏好建模损失为 [CLB+17]

items.en: and for batched sample pairs we take the mean over all pairs. This is clearly not the most natural loss function for some applications; for binary ‘correctness’ it would be better to predict if each example is correct or incorrect, and for multiple choice problems, it might be better to maximize the likelihood for the correct response among all available responses. However, since our primary motivation is preference modeling, we will focus on this formulation unless otherwise noted.
items.zh: 对于批处理的样本对，我们取所有对的平均值。显然，这对于某些应用来说不是最自然的损失函数；对于二分类“正确性”，最好预测每个示例是否正确，而对于多项选择问题，最好最大化所有可用响应中正确响应的似然。然而，由于我们的主要动机是偏好建模，除非另有说明，我们将专注于这种公式。

items.en: In particular, we format all binary discriminators as preference models so that the same architecture can be utilized for both binary and ranked evaluations, which is convenient for studying transfer between them. Given any context C with a binary label A/B (e.g., ‘True/False’, ‘Good/Bad’), we create a preference modeling pair C:A>C:BC:AC:B\texttt{C:A}>\texttt{C:B}, where B denotes the incorrect label, and the colon denotes concatenation.
items.zh: 特别是，我们将所有二分类判别器格式化为偏好模型，以便相同的架构可以用于二分类和排序评估，这便于研究它们之间的迁移。给定任何上下文C及其二分类标签A/B（例如，“真/假”、“好/坏”），我们创建一个偏好建模对 C:A > C:B，其中B表示错误标签，冒号表示拼接。

items.en: We also found that appending a special ‘end-of-context’ token to each sequence to unambiguously delineate the end of passage sometimes improves performance, as discussed in section C.4.
items.zh: 我们还发现，在每个序列后附加一个特殊的“上下文结束”token以明确标记段落结尾有时会提高性能，如附录C.4所述。

items.en: For imitation learning, our training objective is simply the autoregressive language modeling loss on the ‘good’ sequence in each pair—that is, we train the model to imitate ‘good’ behavior. In the notation above, this means that for imitation learning we trained on C:A. We found that applying a mask to train only over the response tokens improved performance significantly, so all our imitation learning results are masked. Furthermore, just to clarify, at training time we sum over negative token log-probs to compute the loss as is typically done, but at evaluation time we average over negative token log-probs to make pairwise comparisons (i.e, a pairwise comparison is accurate if the average negative log-prob for the ‘good’ sample is lower than for the ‘bad’ sample). This significantly improves performance when responses have different lengths.
items.zh: 对于模仿学习，我们的训练目标只是每对中“好”序列的自回归语言建模损失——也就是说，我们训练模型模仿“好”行为。在上面的符号中，这意味着对于模仿学习，我们在C:A上训练。我们发现应用掩码仅训练响应token显著提高了性能，因此我们所有的模仿学习结果都是掩码的。此外，为了澄清，在训练时我们求和负token对数概率来计算损失，这是通常的做法，但在评估时我们平均负token对数概率以进行成对比较（即，如果“好”样本的平均负对数概率低于“坏”样本，则成对比较准确）。当响应具有不同长度时，这显著提高了性能。

full.sec: 3.2 Performance and Scaling Results for Ranked versus Binary Preference Datasets
full.secZh: 3.2 排序与二元偏好数据集的性能与规模结果

items.en: Here we provide a short description of our evaluation datasets, some of which we categorize as ‘ranked’ while others are ‘binary’. In this section, all evaluations involve finetuning on a training set and evaluating on a test set.
items.zh: 这里我们简要描述我们的评估数据集，其中一些我们归类为“排序”，另一些为“二分类”。在本节中，所有评估都涉及在训练集上微调并在测试集上评估。

items.en: For these experiments we collected about 500k python functions with test coverage111111We required that at least half of the lines in the function were executed by a combination of tests in the repo. from public github repos, and split these functions into a training and test set. For each function, we discarded the original implementation (keeping only the function definition and docstring) and generated 8 samples from each code model up to 13B parameters, and tested these samples with all available tests. We then created pairs of correct and incorrect samples for each function, using only model-generated code, to avoid confusing code correctness with the task of human-model discrimination. We compared two training procedures: imitation learning on correct functions, and preference modeling comparing the correct and incorrect functions.
items.zh: 对于这些实验，我们从公共GitHub仓库收集了约50万个具有测试覆盖率的Python函数（我们要求函数中至少一半的行被仓库中的测试组合执行），并将这些函数分为训练集和测试集。对于每个函数，我们丢弃原始实现（仅保留函数定义和文档字符串），并从每个高达13B参数的代码模型生成8个样本，并用所有可用测试测试这些样本。然后，我们为每个函数创建正确和错误样本对，仅使用模型生成的代码，以避免将代码正确性与人类-模型判别任务混淆。我们比较了两种训练过程：在正确函数上的模仿学习，以及比较正确和错误函数的偏好建模。

items.en: Then we evaluated performance on the test set in the following way. We generated 100 samples for each function (using pretrained code models), and ranked them according to both mean per-token log-probs of the IL model, and scores produced by the preference model. Then we evaluated the probability that the top sample among k𝑘k, as ranked by either method, was in fact correct (we derive an unbiased formula in appendix B.6, based on the pass@k estimate from [CTJ+21]). For this we used the same model size for training and test set generation and for ranking samples. Some results are shown in figures 11 and 12.
items.zh: 然后我们按以下方式评估测试集上的性能。我们为每个函数生成100个样本（使用预训练代码模型），并根据IL模型的平均每token对数概率和偏好模型产生的分数对它们进行排名。然后，我们评估根据任一方法排名的前k个样本中顶部样本实际正确的概率（我们在附录B.6中推导了一个无偏公式，基于[CTJ+21]的pass@k估计）。为此，我们使用相同大小的模型进行训练集和测试集生成以及样本排名。一些结果显示在图11和12中。

items.en: Overall we found that preference modeling on this binary discrimination task does not improve very significantly on imitation learning. Both PM and IL are quite similar, overall. These results differ from similar recent experiments on math problem solving [CKB+21], though they trained on thousands of times less data. The difference may be that our imitation learning baseline is much stronger, since even before IL finetuning on Code Correctness specifically, our code models had seen a great deal of on-distribution python code.
items.zh: 总体而言，我们发现对于这个二分类判别任务，偏好建模并没有比模仿学习显著提升。PM和IL总体上非常相似。这些结果与最近关于数学问题求解的类似实验不同 [CKB+21]，尽管他们训练的数据量少数千倍。差异可能在于我们的模仿学习基线更强，因为即使在针对代码正确性进行IL微调之前，我们的代码模型已经见过大量同分布的Python代码。

items.capEn: Figure 11: Here we compare the performance of code correctness discriminators and imitation learning for ranking samples. All models used for a fixed color are the same size – the generator of the discriminator training data, the generator of the test samples, and the preference or imitation learning model used for ranking. The fact that some of these curves are not monotonic represents a robustness failure of preference modeling.
items.capZh: 图11：这里我们比较了代码正确性判别器和模仿学习在样本排名上的性能。用于固定颜色的所有模型大小相同——判别器训练数据的生成器、测试样本的生成器以及用于排名的偏好或模仿学习模型。某些曲线非单调的事实代表了偏好建模的鲁棒性失败。

items.capEn: Figure 12: To create this figure, we generated 100 samples (at T=1𝑇1T=1) from code models. We then ranked these samples using either log-probs from the same model, or using a preference model trained to discriminate correct and incorrect code. The "oracle" line plots optimal ranking where all correct samples are ranked before incorrect ones. We see that imitation learning and preference modeling perform similarly.
items.capZh: 图12：为了创建此图，我们从代码模型生成了100个样本（温度为1）。然后，我们使用来自同一模型的对数概率或训练用于区分正确和错误代码的偏好模型对这些样本进行排名。“oracle”线绘制了所有正确样本排在错误样本之前的最优排名。我们看到模仿学习和偏好建模表现相似。

items.capEn: Figure 13: Similarly to Code Correctness in figure 12, we generated 100 samples (at T=1𝑇1T=1) from pretrained language models. We then ranked these samples using either log-probs from an imitation learning model, or using the scores from a preference model trained to discriminate correct vs. incorrect Lambada completions. Note that for some questions, all the generated answers may be incorrect in which case we default to 0 accuracy. We see that these approaches perform similarly, as we expected since Lambada is a ‘binary’ eval. Lambada performance depends significantly on formatting, as noted in appendix B.4. We also include a line for T=0𝑇0T=0 (argmax) sampling .
items.capZh: 图13：与图12中的代码正确性类似，我们从预训练语言模型生成了100个样本（温度为1）。然后，我们使用来自模仿学习模型的对数概率或训练用于区分正确与错误Lambada补全的偏好模型的分数对这些样本进行排名。注意，对于某些问题，所有生成的答案可能都是错误的，在这种情况下我们默认准确率为0。我们看到这些方法表现相似，正如我们所预期的，因为Lambada是一个“二分类”评估。Lambada性能显著依赖于格式，如附录B.4所述。我们还包含了温度为0（argmax）采样的线。

items.en: We now discuss our evaluations on Lambada [PKL+16]. We used the dataset with original formatting, which differs from that used in GPT-3 [BMR+20]. For imitation learning we simply trained on the correct answers in the training set. For binary discrimination, we sampled answers at T=1𝑇1T=1 from models of various sizes, created up to two pairs of correct and incorrect answers for each prompt, and then trained the discriminator to identify the correct completion. At test time we sampled multiple responses for each question (at temperature T=1𝑇1T=1) and ranked them by either log-probs (for IL) or preference modeling score. The results are shown in figure 13, where we see that imitation learning performs roughly on par with preference modeling. This provides an independent verification of what we found with Code Correctness, though again the imitation learning baseline is very strong, as the Lambada task aligns very well with the language model pre-training objective.
items.zh: 现在我们讨论在Lambada [PKL+16]上的评估。我们使用了原始格式的数据集，这与GPT-3 [BMR+20]中使用的不同。对于模仿学习，我们只需在训练集中的正确答案上训练。对于二分类判别，我们从不同大小的模型以温度为1采样答案，为每个提示创建最多两对正确和错误答案，然后训练判别器识别正确的补全。在测试时，我们为每个问题采样多个响应（温度为1），并根据对数概率（对于IL）或偏好建模分数进行排名。结果显示在图13中，我们看到模仿学习与偏好建模大致相当。这提供了对我们从代码正确性中发现的独立验证，尽管模仿学习基线非常强，因为Lambada任务与语言模型预训练目标非常吻合。

items.en: We also performed a comparison of imitation learning and preference modeling on the HellaSwag [ZHB+19] dataset. This is a multiple choice evaluation on commonsense inference—given an event description, the model is asked to identify the most sensible completion. Although each problem presents only three choices, the desired responses are not uniquely correct, but are merely the most sensible inference among the three options. Thus this task is a form of ranked preference modeling, rather than binary discrimination. In agreement with our expectations, we find that preference modeling scales far better than imitation learning on this dataset, as shown in figure 14.
items.zh: 我们还对HellaSwag [ZHB+19]数据集进行了模仿学习和偏好建模的比较。这是一个关于常识推理的多项选择评估——给定一个事件描述，模型被要求识别最合理的补全。尽管每个问题只提供三个选项，但期望的响应并非唯一正确，而只是三个选项中最合理的推理。因此，这个任务是一种排序偏好建模，而不是二分类判别。与我们的预期一致，我们发现偏好建模在这个数据集上的缩放性能远优于模仿学习，如图14所示。

items.en: Note that while the training data is formatted as multiple choice, we convert the data to binary comparisons by pairing the correct choice with a randomly chosen incorrect choice. It might be possible to improve performance by training on all options, but we did not explore this.
items.zh: 注意，虽然训练数据格式化为多项选择，但我们通过将正确选项与随机选择的错误选项配对，将数据转换为二分类比较。可能可以通过在所有选项上训练来提高性能，但我们没有探索这一点。

items.capEn: Figure 14: Scaling behavior of imitation learning and preference modeling on HellaSwag (ranked) and Learn to Summarize (ranked), showing that PM performs better than IL, as we expect for ranked finetuning evaluations.
items.capZh: 图14：模仿学习和偏好建模在HellaSwag（排序）和Learn to Summarize（排序）上的缩放行为，显示PM优于IL，正如我们对排序微调评估所预期的。

items.en: Preference modeling and RLHF has been applied to the task of generating high-quality summaries of short articles [SOW+20]. We study the associated dataset, which we term ‘Learn to Summarize’. It consists of a collection of articles, where each is accompanied by a pair of summaries that have been ranked by trained human workers. This dataset presents a defining example of a ranked preference modeling task, since there is no clear sense in which any given summary is ‘correct’, but typically among any pair of samples, one will be better than the other. We are especially interested in this finetuning evaluation as it is highly relevant for alignment. We created our own data split by shuffling the data and splitting it into a train (64k pairs) and test (29k pairs) set. On this dataset preference modeling performs far better than imitation learning, as seen in figure 14.
items.zh: 偏好建模和基于人类反馈的强化学习（RLHF）已被应用于生成短文高质量摘要的任务 [SOW+20]。我们研究了相关数据集，将其称为“学习摘要”。该数据集包含一系列文章，每篇文章都附有一对由经过培训的人类工作者排序的摘要。该数据集是排序偏好建模任务的一个典型示例，因为没有任何给定的摘要是“正确”的，但通常在任何一对样本中，一个会比另一个更好。我们特别关注这个微调评估，因为它与对齐高度相关。我们通过打乱数据并将其分为训练集（64k 对）和测试集（29k 对）来创建自己的数据划分。在该数据集上，偏好建模的表现远优于模仿学习，如图 14 所示。

items.en: Ethics (Binary, except for Utilitarianism)
items.zh: 伦理学（二分类，除功利主义外）

items.capEn: Figure 15: Scaling behavior of imitation learning and preference modeling for all five Ethics evaluations, which are all binary except Utilitarianism. We find, in agreement with our expectations, that PM beats IL on the ranked task, but on binary tasks they perform similarly. For brevity we have only included the easier evaluation sets here.
items.capZh: 图 15：所有五个伦理学评估（除功利主义外均为二分类）中模仿学习和偏好建模的缩放行为。我们发现，与预期一致，偏好建模在排序任务上优于模仿学习，但在二分类任务上它们表现相似。为简洁起见，此处仅包含较简单的评估集。

items.en: We studied the Ethics tasks [HBB+21], which include five distinct datasets. We provide a simplified description of each here, but we encourage the interested reader to read the original paper for details:
items.zh: 我们研究了伦理学任务 [HBB+21]，其中包括五个不同的数据集。我们在此对每个数据集进行简要描述，但鼓励感兴趣的读者阅读原始论文以了解详情：

items.en: Commonsense Morality (binary): Assess whether a given action is morally acceptable.
items.zh: 常识道德（二分类）：评估给定行为在道德上是否可接受。

items.en: Deontology (binary): Assess whether a given statement is reasonable on the basis of ‘whether an act is required, permitted, or forbidden according to a set of rules or constraints.’
items.zh: 义务论（二分类）：评估给定陈述是否合理，基于“行为是否根据一套规则或约束被要求、允许或禁止”。

items.en: Justice (binary): Assess whether a given statement is reasonable on the basis of impartiality and desert.
items.zh: 正义（二分类）：评估给定陈述是否基于公正和应得性而合理。

items.en: Virtue (binary): Given a personal trait and a scenario involving a character, assess whether the character expresses that particular trait.
items.zh: 美德（二分类）：给定一个个人特质和一个涉及角色的场景，评估该角色是否表现出该特定特质。

items.en: Utilitarianism (ranked): Given two similar scenarios, rank them by how ‘pleasant’ they are for the character involved.
items.zh: 功利主义（排序）：给定两个相似场景，根据它们对相关角色的“愉悦”程度进行排序。

items.en: In terms of the binary versus ranked121212In some cases this might be altered by changing the objective of the task, but this is our understanding based on the given evaluation metrics [HBB+21] distinction, the first four evaluations are clearly binary since they come with binary labels, while we interpret Utilitarianism as a ranked preference modeling task since ‘pleasantness’ is a ranked quality.
items.zh: 就二分类与排序的区分而言，前四个评估显然是二分类的，因为它们带有二分类标签，而我们将功利主义解释为排序偏好建模任务，因为“愉悦”是一种排序质量。

items.en: Each dataset includes a single training set and two test sets (standard and hard). We train our models on the training sets and evaluate on both test sets during and after training. In all cases we evaluate performance in terms of an accuracy. For Commonsense Morality and Utilitarianism, we use binary accuracy. But for Justice, Deontology and Virtue, the samples are grouped such that a model is accurate on the group only if it gets all responses correct within that group. All our accuracy results follow these requirements. In some cases we also display the preference modeling loss (3.1), as in figure 16, and in that case we simply average over all pairwise comparisons, without any grouping.
items.zh: 每个数据集包括一个训练集和两个测试集（标准版和困难版）。我们在训练集上训练模型，并在训练期间和训练后对两个测试集进行评估。在所有情况下，我们都以准确率来评估性能。对于常识道德和功利主义，我们使用二分类准确率。但对于正义、义务论和美德，样本被分组，模型只有在组内所有回答都正确时才被视为在该组上准确。我们所有的准确率结果都遵循这些要求。在某些情况下，我们还会显示偏好建模损失（3.1），如图 16 所示，此时我们简单地对所有成对比较进行平均，不进行任何分组。

items.en: We find that as claimed, PM performs significantly better than IL on the ranked Utilitarianism evaluation, but that PM and IL perform similarly on all binary evaluations, as shown in figure 15.
items.zh: 我们发现，正如所声称的那样，偏好建模在排序的功利主义评估上显著优于模仿学习，但在所有二分类评估上偏好建模和模仿学习表现相似，如图 15 所示。

full.secZh: 4 偏好模型预训练与迁移

items.en: We saw in section 3 that ranked preference modeling typically performs better than imitation learning, and also often scales better as we increase model size. However, some datasets needed for alignment may be small and expensive to source, since they may require high-quality human feedback. For example, we saw a hint in figure 9 that workers may require detailed instructions to differentiate131313A similar observation was made concerning news articles in [BMR+20]. among models much larger than 10B parameters. Thus we are particularly interested in methods to increase sample efficiency when finetuning on small preference modeling datasets.
items.zh: 我们在第 3 节中看到，排序偏好建模通常优于模仿学习，并且随着模型规模的增大，其缩放效果也往往更好。然而，对齐所需的一些数据集可能规模较小且获取成本高昂，因为它们可能需要高质量的人类反馈。例如，我们在图 9 中看到一些迹象，表明工作人员可能需要详细的指导才能区分远大于 10B 参数的模型。因此，我们特别关注在小型偏好建模数据集上进行微调时提高样本效率的方法。

items.en: In this section we will explore the idea of a ‘preference model pre-training’ (PMP) phase of training, after basic language model (LM) pretraining and before finetuning on a smaller preference modeling dataset relevant for alignment. Our training pipeline can be summarized as
items.zh: 在本节中，我们将探讨在基本语言模型（LM）预训练之后、在与对齐相关的小型偏好建模数据集微调之前，引入“偏好模型预训练”（PMP）阶段的训练想法。我们的训练流程可以概括为：

items.en: Each PMP training dataset typically consists of millions of sequence pairs, while each fine-tuning dataset typically consists of thousands to tens of thousands of sequence pairs.
items.zh: 每个 PMP 训练数据集通常包含数百万个序列对，而每个微调数据集通常包含数千到数万个序列对。

items.en: Training on large public preference modeling data sourced from e.g. Stack Exchange question-answer pairs, Reddit comments, and Wikipedia edits (that revert ‘suspected vandalism’) significantly improves sample efficiency when subsequently finetuning on small preference modeling datasets.
