The pre-training datasets are explained in section 4.1, and the finetuning results are presented in section 4.2.
items.zh: 在大型公开偏好建模数据（例如来自 Stack Exchange 问答对、Reddit 评论和 Wikipedia 编辑（用于撤销“疑似破坏行为”））上进行训练，可以显著提高后续在小型偏好建模数据集上微调时的样本效率。预训练数据集在第 4.1 节中解释，微调结果在第 4.2 节中呈现。

items.en: In particular, we find that each PMP dataset is capable of transfering to a variety of finetuning datasets, with an effect size that seems to grow with model size, even though there may not be any obvious similarities between the datasets.
items.zh: 特别地，我们发现每个 PMP 数据集都能够迁移到各种微调数据集，其效应量似乎随模型规模增大而增大，即使数据集之间可能没有明显的相似性。

items.en: Intriguingly, for the PMP stage of training, it’s most beneficial to train on binary discrimination data rather than ranked preferences. We suspect this is because ranked preferences often need to be ‘unlearned’ during finetuning, which presents a liability to transfer, as explained in section 4.3. In particular, for PMP we apply a simple ‘binarization’ method that converts any ranked PM dataset to binary discrimination, as explained in section 4.1.
items.zh: 有趣的是，在 PMP 训练阶段，在二分类判别数据上训练比在排序偏好数据上训练更有益。我们推测这是因为排序偏好通常需要在微调过程中“被遗忘”，这对迁移构成了一种负担，如第 4.3 节所述。特别是，对于 PMP，我们应用了一种简单的“二值化”方法，将任何排序偏好数据集转换为二分类判别数据，如第 4.1 节所述。

items.capEn: Figure 16: Transfer performance at 500 and 5k finetuning sequence pairs averaged across multiple finetuning evaluations (Learn to Summarize, HellaSwag, and all five Ethics evaluations).
items.capZh: 图 16：在多个微调评估（学习摘要、HellaSwag 和所有五个伦理学评估）上，500 和 5k 微调序列对的平均迁移性能。

items.en: We constructed multiple PMP datasets from various data dumps found online, including StackExchange, Reddit, Wikipedia, and a mixture of all three we refer to as the ‘Mix’. In each case, we began by creating a ranked dataset consisting of pairwise comparisons, with each pair consisting of a ‘better’ and ‘worse’ sample. Details on each dataset is provided in section C.1.
items.zh: 我们从网上找到的各种数据转储中构建了多个 PMP 数据集，包括 StackExchange、Reddit、Wikipedia，以及三者混合的“混合”数据集。在每种情况下，我们首先创建一个由成对比较组成的排序数据集，每对包含一个“更好”和一个“更差”的样本。每个数据集的详细信息见第 C.1 节。

items.en: Subsequently, we created a binary dataset by applying a ‘binarization’ procedure to the ranked dataset. That is, for every ranked pair A>BAB\texttt{A}>\texttt{B}, we transform it into two independent binary comparisons:
items.zh: 随后，我们通过对排序数据集应用“二值化”过程创建了一个二值数据集。也就是说，对于每个排序对 A>B，我们将其转换为两个独立的二值比较：

items.en: Consequently, the binary dataset has twice as many pairs as the ranked dataset. As discussed in more detail in section 4.3, we found that pre-training on the binary dataset typically transferred better than the corresponding ranked version, and so all our PMP experiments assume binary pre-training unless otherwise stated.
items.zh: 因此，二值数据集的样本对数量是排序数据集的两倍。如第4.3节中更详细讨论的那样，我们发现，在二值数据集上进行预训练通常比相应的排序版本迁移效果更好，因此，除非另有说明，我们所有的PMP实验都假设采用二值预训练。

items.capEn: Figure 17: Transfer to various finetuning evaluations from PMP (on the ‘Mix’ pre-training dataset, shown as violet curves) and no PMP (black curves). Each of the five Ethics datasets (Commonsense Morality, Deontology, Justice, Utilitarianism, and Virtue) has both an ‘easy’ test set (solid curves) and a ‘hard’ test set (dashed curves), but only one training set. The x-axis shows the number of finetuning training sequence pairs, while the y-axis shows accuracy as evaluated on a held-out test set. All results are shown for the 52B parameter model. In most cases PMP significantly improves sample efficiency, especially in the ≲10​kless-than-or-similar-toabsent10𝑘\lesssim 10k sequence pairs regime. Plots show 4 training epochs for each eval.
items.capZh: 图17：从PMP（在“混合”预训练数据集上，以紫色曲线显示）和无PMP（黑色曲线）向各种微调评估的迁移。五个伦理数据集（常识道德、道义论、正义、功利主义和美德）各自都有一个“简单”测试集（实线曲线）和一个“困难”测试集（虚线曲线），但只有一个训练集。x轴表示微调训练序列对的数量，y轴表示在保留测试集上评估的准确率。所有结果均针对52B参数模型显示。在大多数情况下，PMP显著提高了样本效率，尤其是在≲10k序列对范围内。每个评估的图表显示了4个训练周期。

items.en: We pre-train a scan of preference models of various sizes on each binary dataset. Training details such as hyperparameter choices are described in section C.1.
items.zh: 我们在每个二值数据集上预训练了一系列不同规模的偏好模型。训练细节（如超参数选择）在第C.1节中描述。

items.en: Here we show finetuning results after preference model pre-training (PMP) on a variety of downstream finetuning evaluations. We find that all our PMP models significantly improve sample efficiency when finetuning, despite there often being little similarity between the PMP distribution and the finetuning distribution.
items.zh: 这里我们展示了在偏好模型预训练（PMP）之后，在各种下游微调评估上的微调结果。我们发现，尽管PMP分布与微调分布之间通常很少有相似性，但我们所有的PMP模型在微调时都显著提高了样本效率。

items.en: Our results are summarized in figure 4, showing the performance gain of PMP. Since performance on all of our final finetuning datasets can be evaluated in terms of accuracy, we define the performance gain as the accuracy difference between PMP and no PMP as measured on each test set. We show the accuracy gain of PMP as a function of number of finetuning sequences, where the pre-training dataset consists of a mixture of StackExchange, Reddit, and Wikipedia which we simply refer to as the ‘Mix’. Furthermore, the lightly shaded violet curves show results for individual finetuning evaluations, while the bold violet curve shows their mean. More detailed breakdown of results is shown in figure 17 and figure 32.
items.zh: 我们的结果总结在图4中，显示了PMP的性能提升。由于我们所有最终微调数据集的性能都可以用准确率来评估，我们将性能提升定义为在每个测试集上测量的PMP与无PMP之间的准确率差异。我们展示了PMP的准确率提升作为微调序列数量的函数，其中预训练数据集由StackExchange、Reddit和Wikipedia的混合组成，我们简称为“混合”。此外，浅紫色曲线显示了单个微调评估的结果，而粗紫色曲线显示了它们的平均值。结果的更详细分解见图17和图32。

items.en: We are also interested in how finetuning scales with model size, especially in the small data limit, as shown in figure 16. We find that at 1k finetuning sequences (or 500 pairs), PMP on the Mix dataset improves performance significantly for models larger than ∼similar-to\sim 1B parameters, but does not appear to benefit small models. Furthermore, at 10k finetuning sequences (or 5000 pairs), PMP Mix also benefits large models, but to a lesser extent. We also show results for scaling of the best-achieved loss with model size on the finetuning evaluation datasets in figure 28 in the appendix.
items.zh: 我们还对微调如何随模型规模扩展感兴趣，特别是在小数据量情况下，如图16所示。我们发现，在1k微调序列（或500对）时，混合数据集上的PMP显著提高了大于~1B参数的模型的性能，但似乎对小模型没有益处。此外，在10k微调序列（或5000对）时，混合PMP仍然有利于大模型，但程度较小。我们还在附录中的图28中展示了微调评估数据集上最佳损失随模型规模扩展的结果。

items.en: As already mentioned, pre-training on binary distributions typically transfers better than ranked distributions—this is discussed more in section 4.3. In addition, we found that the following factors also helped, all of which have been incorporated into our experiments unless otherwise stated:
items.zh: 如前所述，在二值分布上的预训练通常比排序分布迁移得更好——这将在第4.3节中进一步讨论。此外，我们发现以下因素也有帮助，除非另有说明，所有这些因素都已纳入我们的实验：

items.en: Adding to the preference modeling loss a basic language modeling loss to teach the model to imitate the ‘good’ sequence in each preference modeling pair, as discussed in section C.3.
items.zh: 在偏好建模损失中添加一个基本的语言建模损失，以教导模型模仿每个偏好建模对中的“好”序列，如第C.3节所述。

items.en: Appending an end-of-context token to each sequence on top of which the preference modeling score is predicted, as discussed in C.4.
items.zh: 在每个序列后附加一个上下文结束标记，在此基础上预测偏好建模分数，如第C.4节所述。

full.sec: 4.3 Ranked Preference Modeling vs Binary Discrimination for PMP
full.secZh: 4.3 排序偏好建模与二元判别在PMP中的对比

items.capEn: Figure 18: In this figure we show the benefit of ‘binarizing’ PMP datasets; the y-axis is the gain in finetuning accuracy with binarization versus without binarization. The x-axis counts number of text sequences seen by the model, with 2 sequences corresponding to a single preference-modeling comparison.
items.capZh: 图18：在此图中，我们展示了“二值化”PMP数据集的好处；y轴是二值化与未二值化相比微调准确率的增益。x轴表示模型看到的文本序列数量，其中2个序列对应一个偏好建模比较。

items.en: Recall that our pre-training dataset comes in two forms: ranked and binary. So far we have only presented fine-tuning results from binary PMP, but here we also compare to ranked pre-training, and show that binary pre-training typically transfers better than ranked-pre-training. This may be counter-intuitive because preference models are designed to learn an Elo-like score, which can be interpreted as a ranking, and so it is natural to expect ranked pre-training to outperform binary. The goals of this section are to (1) present empirical results showing the difference, and (2) provide and briefly test a plausible explanation.
items.zh: 回想一下，我们的预训练数据集有两种形式：排序和二值。到目前为止，我们只展示了来自二值PMP的微调结果，但这里我们也与排序预训练进行了比较，并表明二值预训练通常比排序预训练迁移得更好。这可能违反直觉，因为偏好模型旨在学习一个类似Elo的分数，可以解释为排名，因此很自然地期望排序预训练优于二值。本节的目标是（1）提供显示差异的实证结果，以及（2）提供并简要测试一个合理的解释。

items.en: In figure 18 we show the advantage of binary pre-training over ranked pre-training. In particular, for each finetuning evaluation, we plot the accuracy difference vs. the number of training sequences, which can be seen as lightly shaded violet curves. Since there is significant variance in these results, we also take the mean over all such evaluations, giving the bold violet curve. On average, we find that binary pre-training performs +5% better at 500 sequence pairs, and +2% better at 5k sequence pairs. More detailed plots of binary vs. ranked pre-training can be found in figure 37 in the appendix, showing the accuracy difference for multiple individual pre-training datasets and multiple individual finetuning evaluations.
items.zh: 在图18中，我们展示了二值预训练相对于排序预训练的优势。特别是，对于每个微调评估，我们绘制了准确率差异与训练序列数量的关系，这可以看作是浅紫色曲线。由于这些结果存在显著方差，我们还取所有此类评估的平均值，得到粗紫色曲线。平均而言，我们发现二值预训练在500序列对时表现好+5%，在5k序列对时好+2%。关于二值与排序预训练的更多详细图可以在附录中的图37中找到，显示了多个单独预训练数据集和多个单独微调评估的准确率差异。

items.en: This result surprised some of the authors, but with hindsight we found a plausible explanation. When pre-training on a ranked dataset, the model learns a corresponding ranked ordering for sample sequences (represented by a scalar value for each sample). However, downstream evaluations may have rankings that are qualitatively very different, which may then require the pre-trained model to ‘unscramble’ its existing ratings. On the contrary, binary pre-training establishes a much less ‘rigid’ score, which may require less ‘unscrambling’ and thus may transfer more easily to very different datasets. We designed an experiment with synthetic data that appears to confirm this hypothesis, which we describe in detail in appendix C.6.
items.zh: 这个结果让一些作者感到惊讶，但事后我们找到了一个合理的解释。当在排序数据集上预训练时，模型学习样本序列的相应排序顺序（由每个样本的标量值表示）。然而，下游评估可能具有性质上非常不同的排序，这可能需要预训练模型“解开”其现有的评分。相反，二值预训练建立了一个“刚性”小得多的分数，可能需要更少的“解开”，因此可能更容易迁移到非常不同的数据集。我们设计了一个使用合成数据的实验，似乎证实了这一假设，我们在附录C.6中详细描述。

full.sec: 4.4 Human-Model vs Human-Human Comparisons for PMP
full.secZh: 4.4 人-模型与人-人比较在PMP中的对比

items.en: All our PMP datasets so far consist of ‘human-human’ comparisons, by which we mean that both samples in each pair are human-written. For this section we consider an alternative dataset consisting of ‘human-model’ comparisons, as we are interested in whether this might improve transfer performance. It is also noteworthy that such comparisons should be easy to generate, since any high-quality fragment of human text might be compared to model-generated text on the same subject.
items.zh: 到目前为止，我们所有的PMP数据集都包含“人类-人类”比较，即每对中的两个样本都是人类编写的。在本节中，我们考虑一个由“人类-模型”比较组成的替代数据集，因为我们感兴趣的是这是否可能提高迁移性能。同样值得注意的是，这种比较应该很容易生成，因为任何高质量的人类文本片段都可以与同一主题的模型生成文本进行比较。

items.en: The ‘human-model’ dataset was created by following these steps:
items.zh: “人类-模型”数据集是通过以下步骤创建的：

items.en: We first finetuned a language model to imitate the ‘good’ samples in our ranked pre-training dataset (e.g., StackExchange, Reddit, or Wikipedia).
items.zh: 我们首先微调了一个语言模型，以模仿排序预训练数据集（例如，StackExchange、Reddit或Wikipedia）中的“好”样本。

items.en: For each sample pair in the ranked pre-training dataset, we kept the ‘good’ sequence, but replaced the “bad” sequence with a sample from the finetuned language model.
items.zh: 对于排序预训练数据集中的每个样本对，我们保留了“好”序列，但将“坏”序列替换为来自微调语言模型的样本。

items.en: Consequently, the resulting dataset has the same number of pairs as the original ranked pre-training dataset, with “good” human-written sequences and “bad” model-written sequences. For these experiments we used the Reddit PMP dataset, and a 3B model for sample generation.
items.zh: 因此，生成的数据集与原始排序预训练数据集具有相同数量的对，其中“好”序列是人类编写的，“坏”序列是模型编写的。在这些实验中，我们使用了Reddit PMP数据集，以及一个用于样本生成的3B模型。

items.en: We found that PMP on the human-model Reddit dataset transfers significantly better to HellaSwag, and somewhat better to Learn to Summarize, as shown in figure 19. Transfer to the Ethics evaluations (see figure 36) is more ambiguous, showing both positive and negative signals. Our suspicion is that human-model pre-training has a particular advantage on downstream finetuning evaluations that contain model-generated data—indeed, all incorrect answers on HellaSwag are model-generated, and Learn to Summarize has a significant amount of model-generated summaries, while Ethics has no model-generated data. Nonetheless, PMP with human-model generated data deserves further investigation, especially since it can be applied to such a great variety of data distributions.
items.zh: 我们发现，在人类-模型Reddit数据集上的PMP显著更好地迁移到HellaSwag，并且在一定程度上更好地迁移到Learn to Summarize，如图19所示。迁移到伦理评估（见图36）则更加模糊，显示出正面和负面信号。我们怀疑，人类-模型预训练在包含模型生成数据的下游微调评估上具有特殊优势——事实上，HellaSwag上的所有错误答案都是模型生成的，Learn to Summarize有大量模型生成的摘要，而伦理没有模型生成的数据。尽管如此，使用人类-模型生成数据的PMP值得进一步研究，特别是因为它可以应用于如此多样的数据分布。

items.capEn: Figure 19: We compare PMP on “human-human” vs “human-model” Reddit datasets by evaluating their transfer performance (for the latter, the “model” pre-training samples were all generated by a 2.7B model). It appears that “human-model” pre-training transfers better on Learn to Summarize and significantly better on HellaSwag, possibly because both evaluations contain model-generated data, thus giving “human-model” an advantage. While our primary focus has been on “human-human”, this results suggests that “human-model” also deserves further investigation.
items.capZh: 图19：我们比较了PMP在“人类-人类”与“人类-模型”Reddit数据集上的迁移性能（对于后者，“模型”预训练样本均由2.7B模型生成）。结果表明，“人类-模型”预训练在“学习摘要”任务上迁移效果更好，在HellaSwag上显著更好，这可能是因为两个评估都包含模型生成的数据，从而给“人类-模型”带来了优势。虽然我们的主要关注点是“人类-人类”，但这一结果表明“人类-模型”也值得进一步研究。

items.en: There have been many works related to AI safety and alignment, including some suggestions for global research plans such as [AOS+16] and [HCSS21]. Work using human feedback to learn summarizations [SOW+20] has particular relevance to our work, since they observe that preference modeling and RL lead to dramatic improvements compared to imitation learning. One of our motivations was to understand when such improvements can be expected from these techniques, and how we can take maximal advantage of human feedback data. To inquire into our models’ alignment we discussed ethics evaluations from [HBB+21], adversarial honesty evaluations from [LHE21], and toxicity evaluations from [GGS+20].
items.zh: 已有许多与AI安全和对齐相关的工作，包括一些全球研究计划的建议，如[AOS+16]和[HCSS21]。使用人类反馈学习摘要的工作[SOW+20]与我们的研究特别相关，因为他们观察到偏好建模和强化学习相比模仿学习带来了显著改进。我们的动机之一是理解何时可以从这些技术中预期到这种改进，以及如何最大化利用人类反馈数据。为了探究我们模型的对齐情况，我们讨论了来自[HBB+21]的伦理评估、[LHE21]的对抗性诚实评估以及[GGS+20]的有毒性评估。

items.en: Our use of a small amount of high-quality data for alignment is most similar to [SD21]. On the other end of the spectrum, a rather different technique is to filter pretraining data, as discussed in [NRA+21]. Our use of prompts was motivated by observations about the behavior of large language models [BMR+20]. Some other observations about prompting and the dependence of prompt-tuning on scale were made in [LARC21] though we did not utilize prompt tuning. The fact that larger models are less subject to forgetting [RDR20] may be related to the fact that larger models do not incur significant alignment taxes.
items.zh: 我们使用少量高质量数据进行对齐的做法与[SD21]最为相似。另一方面，一种截然不同的技术是过滤预训练数据，如[NRA+21]所述。我们使用提示的动机源于对大型语言模型行为的观察[BMR+20]。关于提示以及提示调优对规模依赖性的其他观察在[LARC21]中有所提及，尽管我们没有使用提示调优。较大模型不易遗忘[RDR20]这一事实可能与较大模型不会产生显著对齐税有关。

items.en: Our coding models are similar to those discussed in [CTJ+21]. They also performed alignment-related evaluations, though with high and low quality code examples rather than a natural language prompt. The recent work [AON+21] evaluated language models (without a great deal of code training) on code, including in a conversational manner.
items.zh: 我们的代码模型与[CTJ+21]中讨论的类似。他们也进行了与对齐相关的评估，但使用的是高质量和低质量的代码示例，而非自然语言提示。最近的工作[AON+21]评估了语言模型（没有大量代码训练）在代码上的表现，包括以对话方式。

items.en: Many papers have studied scaling laws [HNA+17, RRBS19, KMH+20, Jon21]. A few have compared discriminators or preference models to imitation learning, including [ILP+18, SOW+20, WOZ+21]. The T-REX IRL method [BGNN19] uses ranked preference modeling to improve on GAIL and on imitation learning. The authors of [AAB+21] compared GAIL [HE16] to conventional imitation learning in an RL context, and found in some cases that GAIL scaled significantly better with dataset size. Experiments comparing RL and behavioral cloning with the decision transformer [CLR+21] are also somewhat similar to our comparison of preference modeling and imitation learning. Very recently [CKB+21] performed experiments that are very similar to our work on code correctness, except that they studied mathematical problem solving, and focused more on dataset size scaling. Interestingly, they find that a verifier (aka binary discriminator) has a more favorable dataset size scaling as compared to imitation learning. However, their experiments are likely in a different regime from ours – they were severely data limited, training on only thousands of math problems, whereas our models were trained on millions of python files, perhaps giving us a much stronger baseline for imitation learning.
items.zh: 许多论文研究了缩放定律[HNA+17, RRBS19, KMH+20, Jon21]。一些论文比较了判别器或偏好模型与模仿学习，包括[ILP+18, SOW+20, WOZ+21]。T-REX IRL方法[BGNN19]使用排序偏好模型改进了GAIL和模仿学习。[AAB+21]的作者在强化学习背景下比较了GAIL[HE16]与传统模仿学习，发现在某些情况下GAIL随数据集大小扩展得更好。使用决策Transformer[CLR+21]比较强化学习和行为克隆的实验也与我们对偏好建模和模仿学习的比较有些相似。最近[CKB+21]进行的实验与我们在代码正确性上的工作非常相似，只是他们研究了数学问题求解，并更关注数据集大小的缩放。有趣的是，他们发现验证器（即二元判别器）相比模仿学习具有更有利的数据集大小缩放。然而，他们的实验可能处于与我们不同的状态——他们严重受限于数据，仅训练了数千个数学问题，而我们的模型在数百万个Python文件上训练，这可能为模仿学习提供了更强的基线。

items.en: Various works [LARC21, WBZ+21, SWR+21, ATS+21] have noted that by finetuning on a large variety of simple tasks, one can improve model performance generally and achieve instruction-following behavior. This idea is closely related to the ‘preference model pre-training’ approach we have discussed. The work with the most similar approach to PMP for alignment was the very recent Delphi [JHB+21], which trains a general-purpose ethical critic. Their work differs insofar as we investigate transfer between distributions that are only distantly related (e.g. from Stack Exchange to summarization), whereas they focus on transfer from and to data related to ethics.
items.zh: 多项工作[LARC21, WBZ+21, SWR+21, ATS+21]指出，通过在大量简单任务上进行微调，可以普遍提高模型性能并实现指令遵循行为。这一想法与我们讨论的“偏好模型预训练”方法密切相关。与PMP对齐方法最相似的工作是最近的Delphi[JHB+21]，它训练了一个通用伦理批评器。他们的工作不同之处在于，我们研究了仅远距离相关的分布之间的迁移（例如从Stack Exchange到摘要），而他们专注于与伦理相关的数据之间的迁移。

items.en: This work was motivated by the problem of technical AI alignment, with the specific goal of training a natural language agent that is helpful, honest, and harmless. We believe this work is important because of the potential for very broad impacts from AI and from language models in particular, especially if progress in the field continues at its current rapid pace [Bow21].
items.zh: 这项工作的动机源于技术性AI对齐问题，具体目标是训练一个有用、诚实且无害的自然语言智能体。我们认为这项工作很重要，因为AI尤其是语言模型可能产生非常广泛的影响，特别是如果该领域继续以当前快速步伐发展[Bow21]。

items.en: We hope that by directly approaching a general and ambitious problem, we will either (1) fail due to specific technical challenges, which we would then attempt to more precisely articulate for further study from the research community, or (2) convince ourselves that we have addressed technical alignment for currently available models.141414Of course, we may fail in uninteresting ways, due to our own limitations, and in that case we can only hope that future work will be more successful. In the event of the second outcome, we would expect our results to be carefully interrogated by the research community. There would also be a need for further empirical investigations into how well these techniques scale to more capable models in terms of both robustness and efficiency, and how likely it is that we will be able to detect alignment failures in more capable models.
items.zh: 我们希望直接解决一个通用且雄心勃勃的问题，要么（1）因特定技术挑战而失败，然后我们将尝试更精确地阐述这些挑战以供研究界进一步研究，要么（2）确信我们已经解决了当前可用模型的技术对齐问题。141414当然，我们可能因自身局限性而以无趣的方式失败，在这种情况下，我们只能希望未来的工作更加成功。如果出现第二种结果，我们期望研究界仔细审视我们的结果。还需要进一步实证研究这些技术如何更好地扩展到更强大的模型（在鲁棒性和效率方面），以及我们检测更强大模型中对齐失败的可能性有多大。

items.en: The road to hell is paved with good intentions, and as such we shouldn’t be complacent with concerns associated with alignment work. Foremost in our minds is that advances in aligning AI with human values do not depend on any specific choice for these values. Efficient alignment techniques could be used to train highly capable systems that do things we consider to be bad, for instance systems for misinformation, censorship, or oppression. Even terms like helpful, honest, and harmless are ambiguous and can be in tension with each other, and it’s easy to imagine them distorted beyond their original meaning, perhaps in intentionally Orwellian ways. And within the context of our own and similar work, the choice of who provides feedback data to train models has broad implications.
items.zh: 通往地狱的道路是由善意铺成的，因此我们不应自满于与对齐工作相关的担忧。我们最关心的是，AI与人类价值观对齐的进展并不依赖于这些价值观的任何特定选择。高效的对齐技术可能被用来训练能够做我们认为坏事的高度能力系统，例如用于虚假信息、审查或压迫的系统。即使像有用、诚实和无害这样的术语也是模糊的，可能相互冲突，并且很容易想象它们被扭曲得超出原意，可能以故意奥威尔式的方式。在我们自己及类似工作的背景下，谁提供反馈数据来训练模型的选择具有广泛影响。

items.en: Information such as our comparisons among different scaling behavior may also be useful for improving AI capabilities, without regard for safety. We believe that understanding how and why ML systems work will be essential to improving their safety, and that these sorts of comparisons aid in that effort. Another concern is that alignment progress might be used as an excuse for carelessness, or to conclude that alignment has already been adequately addressed and can subsequently be ignored. Our view is that people and organizations that deploy AI systems need to take responsibility for their behavior. Research may help to make such deployments possible, but the question of broader relevance is simply whether deployed AI systems are actually safe and beneficial in practice.
items.zh: 诸如我们关于不同缩放行为的比较等信息也可能有助于提高AI能力，而不考虑安全性。我们认为理解机器学习系统的工作原理和原因对于提高其安全性至关重要，而这些比较有助于这一努力。另一个担忧是，对齐进展可能被用作粗心大意的借口，或得出对齐已充分解决并可以忽略的结论。我们的观点是，部署AI系统的个人和组织需要对其行为负责。研究可能有助于使此类部署成为可能，但更广泛的相关性问题仅仅是部署的AI系统在实践中是否真正安全且有益。

items.en: Larger models tend to perform better at most tasks, and there is no reason to expect naive alignment-related tasks to be an exception. In line with these expectations, we find that behavioral alignment tends to improve with model size, with even the simplest conceivable intervention (i.e. prompting) leading larger models to perform better on alignment-relevant evaluations.
items.zh: 较大的模型在大多数任务上往往表现更好，没有理由期望与对齐相关的朴素任务例外。与这些预期一致，我们发现行为对齐随模型规模而改善，即使是最简单的干预（即提示）也能使较大模型在对齐相关评估中表现更好。

items.en: One reason to investigate scaling trends for preference modeling would be to understand how to train better preference models. However, one of our motivations was actually a bit different – it was to set expectations for the scaling of reinforcement learning. We would expect that if it is very difficult for models to learn to recognize favorable outcomes, they will also have difficulty learning to take actions that produce such outcomes. That is, value function performance should tell us something about the likely performance of a trained policy. This logic should become irrefutable when preference models are re-purposed as reward models for RL training. So, given that large gains in both absolute performance and scaling are possible when training ranked preference models, significant progress on alignment may also be possible.
items.zh: 研究偏好建模的缩放趋势的一个原因是了解如何训练更好的偏好模型。然而，我们的动机实际上略有不同——是为了设定对强化学习缩放的预期。我们预期，如果模型很难学会识别有利结果，那么它们也将难以学会采取产生这些结果的动作。也就是说，价值函数性能应该告诉我们关于训练策略可能性能的一些信息。当偏好模型被重新用作强化学习训练的奖励模型时，这一逻辑应变得无可辩驳。因此，鉴于训练排序偏好模型时在绝对性能和缩放方面都可能获得巨大收益，对齐方面也可能取得重大进展。

items.en: Yuntao Bai sourced and curated the PMP data with initial help from Ben Mann, conducted the PMP and finetuning experiments, suggested investigating the distinctions between binary and ranked preference modeling, and suggested several ML improvements for preference modeling.
items.zh: Yuntao Bai 采购并整理了 PMP 数据，最初得到 Ben Mann 的帮助，进行了 PMP 和微调实验，建议研究二元和排序偏好建模之间的区别，并为偏好建模提出了若干机器学习改进。

items.en: Anna Chen conducted experiments on scaling trends for imitation learning versus preference modeling, including on function synthesis (with help from Dawn Drain, Andy Jones, and others). She also conducted the experiments on GAN-type discriminators and many other evaluations, and suggested improvements for preference modeling and code quality.
items.zh: Anna Chen 进行了模仿学习与偏好建模缩放趋势的实验，包括函数合成（在 Dawn Drain、Andy Jones 等人的帮助下）。她还进行了 GAN 型判别器实验和许多其他评估，并为偏好建模和代码质量提出了改进建议。

items.en: Anna and Yuntao collaborated on many experiments and on the training and evaluation code for preference modeling.
items.zh: Anna 和 Yuntao 在许多实验以及偏好建模的训练和评估代码上进行了合作。

items.en: Amanda Askell developed the conceptualization of alignment in terms of helpfulness, honesty, and harmlessness. Amanda produced the initial mockup of the model interface and helped to design and build it. Amanda sourced and trained workers for the interface, conducted our original A/B testing experiments, and provided guidance on evaluations.
items.zh: Amanda Askell 从有用性、诚实性和无害性角度发展了对齐的概念化。Amanda 制作了模型界面的初始模型，并帮助设计和构建了它。Amanda 采购并培训了界面工作人员，进行了我们最初的 A/B 测试实验，并提供了评估指导。

items.en: Ben Mann built most of the human interaction interface and the necessary backend for robust and efficient sampling. Ben led all of our data collection efforts for both language and code data, in collaboration with Danny Hernandez, who has led research on data quality. Ben also contributed to the core language model training infrastructure.
items.zh: Ben Mann 构建了大部分人类交互界面以及稳健高效采样所需的后端。Ben 与 Danny Hernandez（领导数据质量研究）合作，领导了我们所有语言和代码数据的收集工作。Ben 还为核心语言模型训练基础设施做出了贡献。

items.en: Ben, Yuntao, Anna, and Amanda contributed to research and project planning.
items.zh: Ben、Yuntao、Anna 和 Amanda 为研究和项目规划做出了贡献。

items.en: Deep Ganguli proposed, conducted, and analyzed experiments on toxicity (with help from Andy Jones and others) and conducted some of our experiments on alignment taxes. He also contributed to discussions on harms and alignment.
items.zh: Deep Ganguli 提出、进行并分析了毒性实验（在 Andy Jones 等人的帮助下），并进行了我们关于对齐税的一些实验。他还参与了关于危害和对齐的讨论。

items.en: Dawn Drain trained the code models and helped Anna with code evaluations, including with collecting functions with test coverage (with some help from Ben Mann, Andy Jones, and Tom Henighan). Dawn also conducted experiments on alignment taxes with code models.
items.zh: Dawn Drain 训练了代码模型，并帮助 Anna 进行代码评估，包括收集具有测试覆盖率的函数（在 Ben Mann、Andy Jones 和 Tom Henighan 的一些帮助下）。Dawn 还使用代码模型进行了对齐税实验。

items.en: Nicholas Joseph was central to building and maintaining a highly efficient distributed training system for large language models and helped with our sampling infrastructure.
items.zh: Nicholas Joseph 在构建和维护用于大型语言模型的高效分布式训练系统方面发挥了核心作用，并协助了我们的采样基础设施。

items.en: Tom Henighan managed our research cluster, helped build our distributed training system, and did research and experiments on the numerical stability of large language model training. He also helped with ML research on large language models. Nova DasSarma has also helped manage the cluster.
items.zh: Tom Henighan 管理我们的研究集群，帮助构建了分布式训练系统，并对大型语言模型训练的数值稳定性进行了研究和实验。他还协助了大型语言模型的机器学习研究。Nova DasSarma 也协助管理了该集群。

items.en: Andy Jones was central in building our sampling infrastructure. He also provided engineering support to the toxicity experiments, A/B testing infrastructure, distributed training, and code model data collection.
items.zh: Andy Jones 在构建我们的采样基础设施中发挥了核心作用。他还为毒性实验、A/B 测试基础设施、分布式训练和代码模型数据收集提供了工程支持。

items.en: Catherine Olsson contributed crucially to alignment ideas, and provided useful advice for sourcing and training contractors to test our models.
items.zh: Catherine Olsson 在对齐思想方面做出了关键贡献，并为招募和培训测试我们模型的承包商提供了有用的建议。

items.en: Led by Tom Brown in collaboration with Sam McCandlish, much of the technical staff at Anthropic contributed to efficient distributed model training and sampling, the underlying ML, and cluster stability. Core contributors include Nicholas Joseph, Tom Henighan, and Andy Jones. Nelson Elhage, Kamal Ndousse, Zac Hatfield-Dodds, and Ben Mann also contributed to this infrastructure.
items.zh: 由 Tom Brown 领导，与 Sam McCandlish 合作，Anthropic 的大部分技术人员为高效的分布式模型训练和采样、底层机器学习以及集群稳定性做出了贡献。核心贡献者包括 Nicholas Joseph、Tom Henighan 和 Andy Jones。Nelson Elhage、Kamal Ndousse、Zac Hatfield-Dodds 和 Ben Mann 也为该基础设施做出了贡献。

items.en: Catherine Olsson and Jared Kaplan wrote the HHH prompt, and along with Deep Ganguli, Anna Chen, Amanda Askell, and many others wrote most of the alignment evaluations. Jackson Kernion helped improve the alignment evaluations and source workers to interact with our models.
items.zh: Catherine Olsson 和 Jared Kaplan 编写了 HHH 提示，并与 Deep Ganguli、Anna Chen、Amanda Askell 及许多其他人一起编写了大部分对齐评估。Jackson Kernion 帮助改进了对齐评估并招募了与我们的模型交互的工作人员。

items.en: Jared Kaplan, Yuntao Bai, Anna Chen, Amanda Askell, Deep Ganguli, and Ben Mann wrote the paper, with helpful comments from everyone at Anthropic.
items.zh: Jared Kaplan、Yuntao Bai、Anna Chen、Amanda Askell、Deep Ganguli 和 Ben Mann 撰写了论文，并得到了 Anthropic 全体成员的有益评论。

items.en: Dario Amodei, Chris Olah, and Jack Clark contributed expertise and advice throughout the project.
items.zh: Dario Amodei、Chris Olah 和 Jack Clark 在整个项目中贡献了专业知识和建议。

items.en: Sam McCandlish led model pretraining efforts, often in collaboration with Jared Kaplan. Sam also led the overall synthesis of engineering and research efforts.
items.zh: Sam McCandlish 领导了模型预训练工作，通常与 Jared Kaplan 合作。Sam 还领导了工程和研究工作的整体综合。

items.en: Jared Kaplan conceived and led the project. He conducted some initial experiments on preference modeling and many of the experiments on prompting and context distillation.
items.zh: Jared Kaplan 构思并领导了该项目。他进行了一些关于偏好建模的初步实验，以及许多关于提示和上下文蒸馏的实验。

items.en: We thank Daniela Amodei, Jia Yuan Loke, Liane Lovitt, Taylor Rogalski, and Timothy Telleen-Lawton for support with this project, and Sam Bowman, Collin Burns, Ethan Dyer, Owain Evans, David Krueger, Jan Leike, Liane Lovitt, Helen Ngo, and Jeff Wu for comments on the draft. We thank Paul Christiano for helpful discussions.
items.zh: 我们感谢 Daniela Amodei、Jia Yuan Loke、Liane Lovitt、Taylor Rogalski 和 Timothy Telleen-Lawton 对该项目的支持，以及 Sam Bowman、Collin Burns、Ethan Dyer、Owain Evans、David Krueger、Jan Leike、Liane Lovitt、Helen Ngo 和 Jeff Wu 对草稿的评论。我们感谢 Paul Christiano 的有益讨论。

This means that their total non-embedding parameter count is N=12​nlayer​dmodel2≈(1.97×105)​nlayer3𝑁12subscript𝑛layersuperscriptsubscript𝑑model21.97superscript105superscriptsubscript𝑛layer3N=12n_{\mathrm{layer}}d_{\mathrm{model}}^{2}\approx(1.97\times 10^{5})n_{\mathrm{layer}}^{3}. The models have a context window of 8192 tokens with a BPE [SHB15] vocabulary of size nvocab=216subscript𝑛vocabsuperscript216n_{\mathrm{vocab}}=2^{16} trained on a mixture of natural language and python code in a substantially similar manner to GPT-3 [BMR+20] and its precursors [RNSS18, RWC+19].
items.zh: 我们训练的所有仅解码器 [LSP+18] Transformer [VSP+17] 模型都具有固定的纵横比 dmodel/nlayer=128，因为已有研究表明这大致是最优的 [KMH+20]。它们的 MLP 向上投影因子为 4，因此 dff=4dmodel。这意味着它们的总非嵌入参数计数为 N=12nlayer dmodel^2 ≈ (1.97×10^5)nlayer^3。这些模型的上下文窗口为 8192 个 token，使用 BPE [SHB15] 词汇表，词汇量大小为 nvocab=2^16，以与 GPT-3 [BMR+20] 及其前身 [RNSS18, RWC+19] 基本相似的方式在自然语言和 Python 代码的混合数据上进行训练。

items.en: The training dataset is composed of 90% natural language and 10% python code. All components of the NL and code datasets were globally fuzzily deduplicated [BMR+20], and we train for one epoch on all sub-components (i.e. we do not repeat any data). The natural language dataset was composed of 55% heavily filtered common crawl data (220B tokens), 32% internet books (128B tokens), and some smaller distributions including OpenWebText, Wikipedia, Stack Exchange, Arxiv, Legal and Patent documents, Ubuntu-IRC discussion, and movie scripts, most of which we sourced from The Pile [GBB+20].
items.zh: 训练数据集由 90% 的自然语言和 10% 的 Python 代码组成。自然语言和代码数据集的所有组成部分都进行了全局模糊去重 [BMR+20]，并且我们在所有子组成部分上训练一个 epoch（即我们不重复任何数据）。自然语言数据集由 55% 的经过严格过滤的 Common Crawl 数据（220B tokens）、32% 的互联网书籍（128B tokens）以及一些较小的分布组成，包括 OpenWebText、Wikipedia、Stack Exchange、Arxiv、法律和专利文档、Ubuntu-IRC 讨论和电影剧本，其中大部分来自 The Pile [GBB+20]。

items.en: Our code models were further finetuned for 100B tokens on a distribution of python code containing about 45B unique tokens, so for a bit more than two epochs of training.
items.zh: 我们的代码模型进一步在包含约 45B 唯一 token 的 Python 代码分布上微调了 100B tokens，因此训练了略多于两个 epoch。

items.zh: 对于对齐税评估，我们使用了自然适合人类-助手对话的公式。对于 Lambada，我们使用了格式：

items.en: To measure toxicity, we first sample text from our models conditioned on prompts from the RealToxicityPrompts dataset [GGS+20]. Each prompt in this dataset is labeled as either toxic or non-toxic. In order to minimize the computational cost incurred by sampling from large models (especially when primed with the large context), we select a random sample (stratified to have an equal number of toxic and non-toxic prompts) of 111K out of the available ≈100absent100\approx 100K prompts.
items.zh: 为了测量毒性，我们首先以 RealToxicityPrompts 数据集 [GGS+20] 中的提示为条件，从我们的模型中采样文本。该数据集中的每个提示都被标记为有毒或无毒。为了最小化从大型模型（尤其是在使用大上下文提示时）采样的计算成本，我们从可用的约 100K 提示中选择了一个随机样本（分层以确保有毒和无毒提示数量相等），共 111K 个。

items.en: Next, following [GGS+20], we sample for each prompt 252525 sequences of up to 202020 tokens each. We use temperature T=1𝑇1T=1 and top P=0.90𝑃0.90P=0.90. For the prompted and distilled models we make two minor modifications. First, we embellish the RealToxicityPrompt to fit with the human-assistant dialogue as follows:
items.zh: 接下来，根据 [GGS+20]，我们对每个提示采样 25 个序列，每个序列最多 20 个 token。我们使用温度 T=1 和 top P=0.90。对于提示和蒸馏模型，我们做了两个小的修改。首先，我们修饰 RealToxicityPrompt 以适合人类-助手对话，如下所示：

items.en: We simply replace <REALTOXICITYPROMPT> with a prompt from the dataset. Second, we never sample the tokens Human: and - - - - - in order to stop the models from emulating a human’s response to the completed sentence. We found that such emulated human responses (which typically looked like Human: Thanks! This looks great!) significantly decreased toxicity (Figure 22). In fact, emulating human responses had an effect size larger than that of the alignment interventions, which confounded the results.
items.zh: 我们简单地将 <REALTOXICITYPROMPT> 替换为数据集中的一个提示。其次，我们从不采样 token "Human:" 和 "- - - - -"，以阻止模型模仿人类对完成句子的响应。我们发现这种模仿的人类响应（通常看起来像 "Human: Thanks! This looks great!"）显著降低了毒性（图 22）。事实上，模仿人类响应的效应量大于对齐干预的效应量，这混淆了结果。

items.capEn: Figure 22: Average toxicity tends to decrease when prompted (orange) and context distilled (green) models emulate human responses (dashed lines) relative to when when they do not (solid lines). Left: For non-toxic prompts, allowing aligned models to emulate human responses tends to slightly decrease average toxicity. Right: For toxic prompts, allowing aligned models to emulate human responses tends to significantly decrease average toxicity, which dwarfs and confounds the effect of the alignment interventions.
items.capZh: 图22：当提示（橙色）和上下文蒸馏（绿色）模型模仿人类回答（虚线）时，平均毒性往往比不模仿时（实线）降低。左图：对于非毒性提示，允许对齐模型模仿人类回答往往会略微降低平均毒性。右图：对于毒性提示，允许对齐模型模仿人类回答往往会显著降低平均毒性，这远远超过并混淆了对齐干预的效果。

items.en: To measure the toxicity of the model generated text, we used an open source toxicity detector [HU20] that outputs a score, between 00 and 111 with a higher score corresponding to more toxic content. In particular, we used the ’unbiased’ RoBERTa based model, which was trained on data from the Jigsaw Unintended Bias in Toxicity Classification Kaggle competition 151515https://www.kaggle.com/c/jigsaw-unintended-bias-in-toxicity-classification/overview. The model achieves an AUC score of 0.93740.93740.9374 on predicting a human-annotated toxicity label. At the time of writing, the highest leaderboard AUC score is 0.94730.94730.9473. Our usage of this model represents a departure from [GGS+20], and other work on toxicity in language models, which typically rely on the widely used and publicly available Perspective API 161616https://www.perspectiveapi.com/ for toxicity detection. We use the open source toxicity detector purely for ease of implementation. However, we verified that the open source toxicity scores are strongly correlated the Perspective toxicity scores (for the prompts we sampled from RealToxicityPrompts dataset, r=0.829𝑟0.829r=0.829) and that the distributions of toxicity are similar for both toxicity detectors. We will leave a re-analysis of toxicity with the Perspective API for future work, though we do not expect this to significantly affect our main findings.
items.zh: 为了衡量模型生成文本的毒性，我们使用了一个开源毒性检测器 [HU20]，该检测器输出一个介于0到1之间的分数，分数越高表示内容越有毒。具体来说，我们使用了基于'无偏' RoBERTa 的模型，该模型在 Jigsaw Unintended Bias in Toxicity Classification Kaggle 竞赛 151515https://www.kaggle.com/c/jigsaw-unintended-bias-in-toxicity-classification/overview 的数据上训练。该模型在预测人工标注的毒性标签上达到了0.9374的AUC分数。在撰写本文时，排行榜上最高的AUC分数是0.9473。我们使用这个模型与 [GGS+20] 及其他关于语言模型毒性的工作有所不同，后者通常依赖广泛使用且公开可用的 Perspective API 161616https://www.perspectiveapi.com/ 进行毒性检测。我们使用开源毒性检测器纯粹是为了实现方便。然而，我们验证了开源毒性分数与 Perspective 毒性分数高度相关（对于我们从 RealToxicityPrompts 数据集中采样的提示，r=0.829），并且两种毒性检测器的毒性分布相似。我们将把使用 Perspective API 重新分析毒性留作未来工作，尽管我们不认为这会显著影响我们的主要发现。

items.en: In Figure 8 we report the mean toxicity score averaged across all 500500500 prompts and 252525 samples per prompt. This represents a departure from [GGS+20] and other work on toxicity in language models, which typically report the metrics: Expected Maximum Toxicity and Probability of Toxicity. The Expected Maximum Toxicity metric reports the maximum toxicity across the 252525 continuations per prompt, averaged across all 500500500 prompts. The probability of toxicity metric captures the average, across prompts, of an indicator variable that’s 111 if a given sample has a toxicity score > 0.50.50.5, and 00 otherwise, across continuations. We report these metrics in Figure 23. We note that, in general, likely due to the maximum and thresholding operations of each metric prior to averaging, both metrics have large standard deviations and do not scale smoothly with model size. Regardless, the general findings from the main text remain true: both context distillation and prompting reduce toxicity and the reduction in toxicity according to these metrics is greater as models get larger. We also observe that both Expected Maximum Toxicity and Probability of Toxicity tend to be strongly correlated with each other.
items.zh: 在图8中，我们报告了所有500个提示和每个提示25个样本的平均毒性分数。这与 [GGS+20] 及其他关于语言模型毒性的工作有所不同，后者通常报告指标：预期最大毒性和毒性概率。预期最大毒性指标报告每个提示的25个延续中的最大毒性，然后对所有500个提示取平均。毒性概率指标捕获的是，对于每个提示，如果给定样本的毒性分数 > 0.5，则指示变量为1，否则为0，然后对所有延续取平均。我们在图23中报告了这些指标。我们注意到，一般来说，由于每个指标在平均之前进行了最大值和阈值操作，这两个指标的标准差较大，并且不随模型大小平滑缩放。尽管如此，正文中的总体发现仍然成立：上下文蒸馏和提示都降低了毒性，并且根据这些指标，毒性降低的程度随着模型变大而增大。我们还观察到，预期最大毒性和毒性概率往往彼此高度相关。

items.capEn: Figure 23: Expected Maximum Toxicity (top plots) and Probability of Toxicity (bottom plots) tend to scale less smoothly with model size in response to both non-toxic (left plots) and toxic (right plots) prompts. Top Left: Expected Maximum Toxicity in response to non-toxic prompts is generally lower for both prompted (orange) and context distilled (green) models relative to unaligned (blue) models. Top Right: Expected Maximum Toxicity in response to toxic prompts only decreases relative to unaligned models only for larger models and increases otherwise. Bottom Left: Probability of Toxicity in response to non-toxic prompts exhibits the same general trend as Expected Maximum Toxicity in response to non-toxic prompts Bottom Right: Probability of Toxicity in response to toxic prompts also exhibits same general trend as Expected Maximum Toxicity.
items.capZh: 图23：预期最大毒性（上图）和毒性概率（下图）在响应非毒性（左图）和毒性（右图）提示时，随模型大小的缩放不太平滑。左上：对于非毒性提示，提示（橙色）和上下文蒸馏（绿色）模型的预期最大毒性通常低于未对齐（蓝色）模型。右上：对于毒性提示，预期最大毒性仅对较大模型相对于未对齐模型降低，否则增加。左下：对于非毒性提示，毒性概率表现出与预期最大毒性相同的总体趋势。右下：对于毒性提示，毒性概率也表现出与预期最大毒性相同的总体趋势。

items.en: To gain intuition about why the simple average toxicity score scales smoothly with model size, we inspect the probability distribution of toxicity scores across model sizes for the base language model (LM, Figure 24 Left). The distribution is bimodal with one peak for low toxicity scores and and a relatively smaller peak for high toxicity scores. As the model size increases, probability mass tends to shift smoothly from the low toxicity peak to the high toxicity peak. Computing the mean of these distributions captures this smooth transition in mass between modes. We also inspect the influence of the alignment interventions for the largest 505050B parameter model (Figure 24 Right). We see that the alignment interventions tend to undo the effect of scaling up model sizes in that they shift probability mass away from the toxic mode towards the less toxic mode.
items.zh: 为了理解为什么简单的平均毒性分数随模型大小平滑缩放，我们检查了基础语言模型（LM，图24左）在不同模型大小下的毒性分数概率分布。该分布是双峰的，一个峰对应低毒性分数，另一个相对较小的峰对应高毒性分数。随着模型大小增加，概率质量往往从低毒性峰平滑转移到高毒性峰。计算这些分布的均值捕捉到了这种模式之间质量的平滑转移。我们还检查了最大500亿参数模型的对齐干预效果（图24右）。我们看到，对齐干预往往抵消了模型规模扩大的效果，即将概率质量从毒性模式转移到较低毒性模式。

items.capEn: Figure 24: The distribution, estimated via kernel density estimation with a Gaussian kernel, of toxicity scores is bimodal, with one peak for for low toxicity scores and a relatively smaller peak for high toxicity scores. Left: For a standard LM, as the model size increases, probability mass tends to shift from the low toxicity peak to the high peak. Right: Conversely, for a 505050B parameter model (blue), prompting (orange) and context distillation (green) tends to shift mass from the high peak to the low peak.
items.capZh: 图24：通过高斯核密度估计得到的毒性分数分布是双峰的，一个峰对应低毒性分数，另一个相对较小的峰对应高毒性分数。左图：对于标准LM，随着模型大小增加，概率质量往往从低毒性峰转移到高毒性峰。右图：相反，对于500亿参数模型（蓝色），提示（橙色）和上下文蒸馏（绿色）往往将质量从高毒性峰转移到低毒性峰。

With our pure language models (no prompt or context distillation), we tried using both this format and even simpler format <QUESTION> <ANSWER>, and found that the latter did very slightly better, and so we have used results from that format in all figures.
