full.sec: B.4 A Comment on Lambada Formatting

items.en: We performed a fairly complicated evaluation on Lambada in section 3.2, which involved finetuning on the training set. Therefore, we used the official version of the dataset, which has a number of typos and strange whitespace and punctuation choices. However, in section 2.2.4 we included some zero-shot Lambada evaluations to assess ‘alignment taxes’. These formatting choices make a very large difference in performance, as shown in figure 25. In particular, we believe this explains in large part why the results from figure 13 are comparatively weak.
items.zh: 我们在第3.2节中对 Lambada 进行了相当复杂的评估，涉及在训练集上进行微调。因此，我们使用了数据集的官方版本，其中包含许多拼写错误以及奇怪的空白和标点选择。然而，在第2.2.4节中，我们包含了一些零样本 Lambada 评估以评估'对齐税'。这些格式选择对性能影响很大，如图25所示。特别是，我们认为这在很大程度上解释了图13中结果相对较弱的原因。

items.en: To be explicit, here is an example from the nicely formatted version:
items.zh: 明确地说，以下是格式良好的版本中的一个示例：

items.en: "Helen’s heart broke a little in the face of Miss Mabel’s selfless courage. She thought that because she was old, her life was of less value than the others. For all Helen knew, Miss Mabel had a lot more years to live than she did. "Not going to happen," replied Helen
items.zh: "Helen's heart broke a little in the face of Miss Mabel's selfless courage. She thought that because she was old, her life was of less value than the others. For all Helen knew, Miss Mabel had a lot more years to live than she did. "Not going to happen," replied Helen"

items.en: And here’s an example from the original version:
items.zh: 以下是原始版本中的一个示例：

items.en: it was very freeing . there would be no more hiding , no more tiptoeing around the conversation . logan and i were together . plain and simple . we cared for each other and were doing what felt right . that did n’t stop my stomach from sinking the second door swung open . dr. andrews strode into the room , casting a cautionary glance in my direction before turning his attention to logan
items.zh: it was very freeing . there would be no more hiding , no more tiptoeing around the conversation . logan and i were together . plain and simple . we cared for each other and were doing what felt right . that did n't stop my stomach from sinking the second door swung open . dr. andrews strode into the room , casting a cautionary glance in my direction before turning his attention to logan

items.en: The difference in performance between these formats might be regarded as an alignment failure itself. For this reason, we were interested in whether the HHH prompt reduced the gap between Lambada formats, but we did not find this effect.
items.zh: 这些格式之间的性能差异本身可能被视为一种对齐失败。因此，我们感兴趣的是 HHH 提示是否缩小了 Lambada 格式之间的差距，但我们没有发现这种效果。

items.capEn: Figure 25: We show Lambada results with three different formats – an awkward format from the original/official Lambada dataset, a format constructed by OpenAI, and a fill-in-the-blanks format used with GPT-3 [BMR+20] that performs very well with few-shot learning.
items.capZh: 图25：我们展示了三种不同格式的 Lambada 结果——原始/官方 Lambada 数据集的笨拙格式、OpenAI 构建的格式，以及 GPT-3 [BMR+20] 使用的填空格式，该格式在少样本学习中表现非常好。

items.en: To perform context distillation in practice, we prepended both the HHH prompt and then Human: (signifying the beginnning of a new conversation) to text samples. We then performed a forward pass with the 52B model and stored the top 50 log-probabilities for each token, along with their indices within the vocabulary. We used a half-and-half mixture of generic pretraining data and Stack Exchange questions. We formatted the latter to use the Assistant: label before the answers, as an attempt to stay near the human-assistant distribution. We filled out the remainder of the context with distillation data, providing about 1500 tokens per sequence (subtracting the length of the prompt).
items.zh: 为了在实践中进行上下文蒸馏，我们将 HHH 提示和 Human：（表示新对话的开始）前置到文本样本前。然后，我们对 52B 模型进行前向传播，并存储每个词元的前50个对数概率及其在词汇表中的索引。我们使用了通用预训练数据和 Stack Exchange 问题的半对半混合。我们将后者格式化为在答案前使用 Assistant：标签，以尝试保持在人机对话分布附近。我们用蒸馏数据填充上下文的其余部分，每个序列提供约1500个词元（减去提示的长度）。

items.en: After generating this data, we finetuned all model sizes on it with KL loss between the stored log-probabilities and the model-predicted probabilities. Since we only stored the top 50 log-probs, for each token this KL was actually a 51-category comparison, with the extra category coming from the aggregation of all other possibilities besides the top 50 from the prompted 52B model.
items.zh: 生成这些数据后，我们在所有模型大小上使用 KL 损失进行微调，损失基于存储的对数概率和模型预测的概率。由于我们只存储了前50个对数概率，对于每个词元，这个 KL 实际上是51类比较，额外的类别来自提示后的52B模型前50个之外的所有其他可能性的聚合。

items.capEn: Figure 26: Left: Per-token losses when counting, along with Laplace’s prediction that “if the sun has risen n𝑛n times in a row, the probability it will not rise tomorrow ∼1/nsimilar-toabsent1𝑛\sim 1/n”. Right: An extreme illustration of the difference between prompting (conditioning) and finetuning (altering the expected data distribution). The finetuned models were trained on the sequence 1,2, …, 63. With sufficient finetuning these models become very confident about counting, but never learn that the first few tokens should be 64, 65, …
items.capZh: 图26：左图：计数时的每个词元损失，以及拉普拉斯的预测“如果太阳连续升起 n 次，明天不升起的概率 ∼ 1/n”。右图：提示（条件化）和微调（改变期望数据分布）之间差异的极端说明。微调模型在序列 1,2, …, 63 上训练。经过充分微调，这些模型对计数变得非常自信，但从未学会前几个词元应该是 64, 65, …

full.sec: B.6 Estimator of Accuracy When Re-Ranking Samples
full.secZh: B.6 重排序样本时准确率的估计

items.en: When studying the performance of models that rank sample quality (with a PM or log-probs from a language model), we’re interested in the measuring the fraction of problems that are solved by the the top-ranked sample, when there are k𝑘k samples in total. Here we derive an unbiased estimator for this quantity when using a finite pool of N≥k𝑁𝑘N\geq k samples. The analysis builds on estimates for pass@k from [CTJ+21].
items.zh: 当研究对样本质量进行排序的模型（使用 PM 或语言模型的对数概率）的性能时，我们感兴趣的是衡量当总共有 k 个样本时，排名最高的样本解决的问题比例。这里我们推导了当使用有限池 N ≥ k 个样本时该数量的无偏估计量。该分析基于 [CTJ+21] 中 pass@k 的估计。

items.en: For each problem, we sample a list of N𝑁N samples, and then calculate both the score for ranking (by a model of interest) and whether each individual response was correct. Then for each problem, we estimate:
items.zh: 对于每个问题，我们采样一个包含 N 个样本的列表，然后计算排序分数（由感兴趣的模型给出）以及每个单独回答是否正确。然后对于每个问题，我们估计：

items.en: where S𝑆S is a list of ranked samples, from better to worse scores; a​c​c​(⋅)𝑎𝑐𝑐⋅acc(\cdot) is the accuracy of the sample (correct or incorrect, so these are all 1 or 0); (Nk)binomial𝑁𝑘\binom{N}{k} is the total number of possible combinations when choosing k𝑘k of N𝑁N samples. Then, crucially, (N−ik−1)binomial𝑁𝑖𝑘1\binom{N-i}{k-1} is the number of combinations where sample sisubscript𝑠𝑖s_{i} is the top-ranked sample among the k𝑘k chosen samples. So the ratio of binomial coefficients in equation (B.6) is the probability that the i𝑖ith sample is chosen and is the highest ranked sample in a group of k𝑘k.
items.zh: 其中 S 是按得分从高到低排序的样本列表；acc(·) 是样本的准确率（正确或错误，因此取值均为 1 或 0）；(N k) 是从 N 个样本中选取 k 个的所有可能组合数。关键在于，(N-i k-1) 是样本 s_i 在所选 k 个样本中排名最高的组合数。因此，公式 (B.6) 中二项式系数的比值即为第 i 个样本被选中且成为 k 个样本中最高排名样本的概率。

items.en: The overall metric is simply the mean of this quantity over all the problems.
items.zh: 整体指标就是所有问题上该量的平均值。

items.capEn: Figure 27: Scaling laws for PMP, showing PM loss vs. model size, for each of the three pre-training datasets StackExchange, Reddit, and Wikipedia, as evaluated on a held-out test set after one training epoch. The “Mix” is simply a mixture consisting of one epoch each of the three pre-training datasets. We do not know why the 52B seems to be off-trend. This could be caused by (1) being in a data-limited regime, or (2) being limited by the entropy of the pre-training distribution, or (3) sub-optimal choice of hyperparameters. Nonetheless, it is interesting to observe (e.g., from figure 5) that the 52B still transfers significantly better than the smaller models.
items.capZh: 图 27：PMP 的缩放定律，展示了 PM 损失与模型大小的关系，针对三个预训练数据集 StackExchange、Reddit 和 Wikipedia，在一个训练 epoch 后在保留测试集上评估。“Mix” 是这三个预训练数据集各一个 epoch 的简单混合。我们不知道为何 52B 模型似乎偏离趋势。这可能是由以下原因造成的：(1) 处于数据受限状态，(2) 受预训练分布熵的限制，或 (3) 超参数选择欠佳。尽管如此，有趣的是（例如从图 5 中观察到）52B 模型的迁移效果仍然显著优于较小的模型。

items.capEn: Figure 28: Scaling trends with model size for the best achieved comparison test loss on various final finetuning evaluations. We have grouped datasets together based on the dynamic range in the loss. The results are measured after one training epoch each for Learn to Summarize and Hellaswag, and four training epochs for each Ethics eval. In all cases larger models perform better, as expected. Sometimes we see a fairly clean power-law trend in the loss, but often there are significant deviations, including perhaps an interesting improvement in the slope on some datasets in the hundred million parameter range.
items.capZh: 图 28：模型大小与各种最终微调评估上最佳比较测试损失的缩放趋势。我们根据损失动态范围对数据集进行了分组。结果是在 Learn to Summarize 和 HellaSwag 上各训练一个 epoch，以及每个 Ethics 评估训练四个 epoch 后测量的。在所有情况下，较大的模型表现更好，符合预期。有时我们观察到损失呈现相当清晰的幂律趋势，但经常存在显著偏差，包括在某些数据集上亿参数范围内斜率可能有趣的改善。

items.en: We now describe how the ranked pre-training datasets were prepared for each domain. The binarization procedure outlined in 4.1 was subsequently applied to convert each ranked dataset to a binary one.
items.zh: 我们现在描述如何为每个领域准备排序的预训练数据集。随后应用第 4.1 节中概述的二值化过程，将每个排序数据集转换为二值数据集。

items.en: StackExchange: The StackExchange Data Dump171717https://archive.org/details/stackexchange consists of questions and answers from the StackExchange website. For each question, we evaluate the ‘score’ of all answers, where the score is defined as the log2⁡(1+upvotes)subscript21upvotes\log_{2}(1+\mathrm{upvotes}) rounded to the nearest integer, plus 111 if the answer was accepted by the questioner (we assign a score of −11-1 if the number of upvotes is negative). In order to make pairwise comparison data for PMP, we sample two answers with distinct scores, skipping questions where this is not possible. For each question-answer pair, the corresponding context is formatted as
items.zh: StackExchange：StackExchange 数据转储（https://archive.org/details/stackexchange）包含来自 StackExchange 网站的问题和答案。对于每个问题，我们评估所有答案的“得分”，其中得分定义为 log2(1+upvotes) 四舍五入到最接近的整数，如果答案被提问者接受则再加 1（如果 upvotes 数为负数，我们分配得分为 -1）。为了生成 PMP 的成对比较数据，我们采样两个得分不同的答案，跳过无法做到这一点的题目。对于每个问题-答案对，相应的上下文格式为

items.en: We prepared 5.8M training pairs and 59k test pairs.
items.zh: 我们准备了 580 万训练对和 5.9 万测试对。

items.en: Reddit: The Pushshift Reddit data dump181818https://files.pushshift.io/reddit/ consists of posts and comments from the Reddit website. For each Reddit post, we sample a pair of comment sequences differing only in the final comment. We then label the sequence whose final comment has the higher number of user upvotes as the “better” sequence, thus forming a preference modeling pair. For each post and comment sequence, the context is formatted as
items.zh: Reddit：Pushshift Reddit 数据转储（https://files.pushshift.io/reddit/）包含来自 Reddit 网站的帖子和评论。对于每个 Reddit 帖子，我们采样一对仅在最后一条评论上不同的评论序列。然后，我们将最后一条评论获得更多用户 upvotes 的序列标记为“更好”的序列，从而形成一个偏好建模对。对于每个帖子和评论序列，上下文格式为

items.en: where each username is replaced with the corresponding author’s alias. We also removed deleted comments and comments from bots. We prepared 1.1M training pairs and 11k test pairs.
items.zh: 其中每个用户名被替换为相应作者的别名。我们还删除了已删除的评论和来自机器人的评论。我们准备了 110 万训练对和 1.1 万测试对。

items.en: Note: We also made an effort to filter away poor or irrelevant data. For instance, we restrict to a “whitelist” of subreddits that we believe have the highest data quality. We specifically chose not to include AmItheAsshole, as it overlaps with one of our fine-tuning datasets, Commonsense Morality. Instead we include the subreddits: tifu, explainlikeimfive, WritingPrompts, changemyview, LifeProTips, todayilearned, science, askscience, ifyoulikeblank, UpliftingNews, Foodforthought, IWantToLearn, bestof, IAmA, socialskills, relationship_advice, philosophy, YouShouldKnow, history, books, Showerthoughts, personalfinance, buildapc, EatCheapAndHealthy, boardgames, malefashionadvice, femalefashionadvice, scifi, Fantasy, Games, bodyweightfitness, SkincareAddiction, podcasts, suggestmeabook, AskHistorians, gaming, DIY, mildlyinteresting, sports, space, gadgets, Documentaries, GetMotivated, UpliftingNews, technology, Fitness, travel, lifehacks, Damnthatsinteresting, gardening, programming.
items.zh: 注意：我们还努力过滤掉质量差或不相关的数据。例如，我们限制在一个我们认为数据质量最高的子版块“白名单”中。我们特意选择不包含 AmItheAsshole，因为它与我们的微调数据集之一 Commonsense Morality 重叠。相反，我们包含的子版块有：tifu、explainlikeimfive、WritingPrompts、changemyview、LifeProTips、todayilearned、science、askscience、ifyoulikeblank、UpliftingNews、Foodforthought、IWantToLearn、bestof、IAmA、socialskills、relationship_advice、philosophy、YouShouldKnow、history、books、Showerthoughts、personalfinance、buildapc、EatCheapAndHealthy、boardgames、malefashionadvice、femalefashionadvice、scifi、Fantasy、Games、bodyweightfitness、SkincareAddiction、podcasts、suggestmeabook、AskHistorians、gaming、DIY、mildlyinteresting、sports、space、gadgets、Documentaries、GetMotivated、UpliftingNews、technology、Fitness、travel、lifehacks、Damnthatsinteresting、gardening、programming。

items.en: Wikipedia: Wikipedia provides a data dump191919https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Database_download of the full edit history for every page. For some edits, a short explanation of the intention behind the edit is provided in the metadata. In particular, a significant number of edits revert “suspected vandalism”, as noted in comments associated with the edits. Examples of vandalism include edits that are intended to be misleading, counterfactual, or irrelevant to the subject matter of the page. For each such edit, we form a preference modeling pair by extracting the contents of the page before and after the edit, with the reverted version labeled as “better”. For each edit, we restrict to only the page sections that had been edited, and make a preference modeling pair for each such section, thus reducing the necessary context length significantly. For each item in each pair, the context simply consists of the contents of the relevant section, formatted as
items.zh: Wikipedia：Wikipedia 提供了一个数据转储（https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Database_download），包含每个页面的完整编辑历史。对于某些编辑，元数据中提供了编辑意图的简短说明。特别是，大量编辑会撤销“疑似破坏行为”，如与编辑相关的评论中所述。破坏行为的示例包括旨在误导、反事实或与页面主题无关的编辑。对于每个此类编辑，我们通过提取编辑前后页面的内容来形成一个偏好建模对，并将回滚版本标记为“更好”。对于每个编辑，我们只限制在被编辑的页面部分，并为每个这样的部分制作一个偏好建模对，从而显著减少所需的上下文长度。对于每个对中的每个项目，上下文仅由相关部分的内容组成，格式为

items.en: We also made an effort to clean out various irrelevant metadata, such as hyperlinks, citations, data tables, and placeholders for images. We also removed uninteresting sections such as references and bibliography. We made 1.4M training pairs and 14k test pairs.
items.zh: 我们还努力清理了各种无关的元数据，例如超链接、引用、数据表和图像占位符。我们还删除了不感兴趣的部分，如参考文献和参考书目。我们制作了 140 万训练对和 1.4 万测试对。

items.en: Mix: We also consider a mixture (i.e., union) of StackExchange, Reddit, and Wikipedia, and refer to it as the “Mix”. Since we choose to use a single epoch of each component dataset, the mix is about 70% StackExchange.
items.zh: Mix：我们还考虑了一个混合（即并集）数据集，包含 StackExchange、Reddit 和 Wikipedia，并称之为“Mix”。由于我们选择每个组成数据集只使用一个 epoch，因此 Mix 中约 70% 是 StackExchange。

items.en: For each pre-training dataset, including the “Mix”, we trained a scan a model sizes for exactly one epoch each. In all cases we used context size of 1024 tokens per sequence, batch size of 512 sequence pairs, and constant learning rate of 0.1 relative to language model pre-training. We evaluate preference model pre-training by PM accuracy (i.e., does the PM assign a higher score to the “good” sample in each pair?) and PM loss (3.1).
items.zh: 对于每个预训练数据集，包括“Mix”，我们训练并扫描了各种模型大小，每个恰好一个 epoch。在所有情况下，我们使用的上下文大小为每个序列 1024 个 token，批大小为 512 个序列对，学习率恒定为语言模型预训练学习率的 0.1 倍。我们通过 PM 准确率（即 PM 是否为每个对中的“好”样本分配更高的分数？）和 PM 损失（3.1）来评估偏好模型预训练。

items.en: We present more detailed finetuning results in figure 17, showing performance as a function of number of finetuning sequence pairs for both PMP (on the Mix dataset) and no PMP.
items.zh: 我们在图 17 中展示了更详细的微调结果，显示了性能与微调序列对数量的关系，包括 PMP（在 Mix 数据集上）和无 PMP 的情况。

items.en: For all these experiments, we used a model context size of 1024 tokens per sequence, batch size of 32 sequence pairs, and a constant learning rate of 0.01 relative to pre-training. We trained for one epoch each on Learn to Summarize and HellaSwag, and four epochs each on the Ethics evaluations as doing so improved performance. We used hyperparameters (λ,μ)=(1,1)𝜆𝜇11(\lambda,\mu)=(1,1) for the PM and LM losses, respectively, as discussed in section C.3.
items.zh: 对于所有这些实验，我们使用的模型上下文大小为每个序列 1024 个 token，批大小为 32 个序列对，学习率恒定为预训练学习率的 0.01 倍。我们在 Learn to Summarize 和 HellaSwag 上各训练一个 epoch，在 Ethics 评估上各训练四个 epoch，因为这样做提高了性能。我们使用的超参数 (λ, μ) = (1, 1) 分别对应 PM 和 LM 损失，如第 C.3 节所述。

full.sec: C.3 Language Modeling Improves PMP Transfer
full.secZh: C.3 语言建模提升PMP迁移

items.capEn: Figure 29: PMP using only the PM loss transfers poorly to downstream evaluations and is typically worse than simply doing no such pre-training at all. However, when combined with an autoregressive language modeling loss that imitates the “good” sample in each training pair, it significantly improves transfer to many downstream evaluations. Here we show results for PMP on Reddit finetuned on Learn to Summarize and HellaSwag, but we made similar observations on all other pre-training and finetuning datasets. Furthermore, the fact that “PM+LM Loss” clearly performs better than “LM Loss Only” strongly suggests that the performance gain of the former does not arise solely from language modeling, but from its combination with preference modeling.
items.capZh: 图 29：仅使用 PM 损失的 PMP 迁移到下游评估时效果较差，通常比完全不进行此类预训练更差。然而，当与自回归语言建模损失（模仿每个训练对中的“好”样本）结合时，它显著改善了对许多下游评估的迁移。这里我们展示了在 Reddit 上进行 PMP 并在 Learn to Summarize 和 HellaSwag 上微调的结果，但我们在所有其他预训练和微调数据集上也观察到了类似情况。此外，“PM+LM Loss”明显优于“LM Loss Only”这一事实强烈表明，前者的性能提升并非仅来自语言建模，而是来自其与偏好建模的结合。

items.capEn: Figure 30: Here we show calibration curves on the summarization test set. We see that aside from the smallest model, the preference models are very well calibrated on-distribution. These models were all first prefence model pre-trained on the stack exchange and then finetuned on summarization PMing. We include a black line as a reference for perfect calibration.
items.capZh: 图 30：这里我们展示了在摘要测试集上的校准曲线。我们看到，除了最小的模型外，偏好模型在分布内校准得很好。这些模型首先在 Stack Exchange 上进行偏好模型预训练，然后在摘要 PMing 上进行微调。我们包含了一条黑色线作为完美校准的参考。

items.en: In this section we describe a technical detail which improves the transfer-ability of PMP significantly. We consider two losses for the pre-training stage: (1) the preference modeling (PM) loss, and (2) an autoregressive language modeling (LM) loss that imitates the “good” sample in each sequence pair.
items.zh: 在本节中，我们描述了一个技术细节，该细节显著提高了 PMP 的迁移能力。我们考虑了预训练阶段的两种损失：(1) 偏好建模（PM）损失，和 (2) 自回归语言建模（LM）损失，该损失模仿每个序列对中的“好”样本。

items.en: where λ,μ𝜆𝜇\lambda,\mu are hyperparameters. For the latter, we do not apply any masking on the tokens and simply train the model to predict the full context of the good sample.
items.zh: 其中 λ, μ 是超参数。对于后者，我们不应用任何 token 掩码，而是直接训练模型预测好样本的完整上下文。

items.en: We found that adding the language modeling loss during pre-training consistently improved the sample efficiency on finetuning evaluations. In figure 29, we show the transfer performance for several pre-training losses:
items.zh: 我们发现，在预训练期间加入语言建模损失，持续提高了微调评估中的样本效率。在图29中，我们展示了多种预训练损失的迁移性能：

items.en: “Pure” PM loss for which (λ,μ)=(1,0)𝜆𝜇10(\lambda,\mu)=(1,0),
items.zh: “纯”PM损失，其中(λ,μ)=(1,0)，

items.en: “Pure” LM loss for which (λ,μ)=(0,1)𝜆𝜇01(\lambda,\mu)=(0,1),
items.zh: “纯”LM损失，其中(λ,μ)=(0,1)，

items.en: “Composite” PM+LM loss for which (λ,μ)=(1,1)𝜆𝜇11(\lambda,\mu)=(1,1),
items.zh: “复合”PM+LM损失，其中(λ,μ)=(1,1)，

items.en: For uniformity, we used (λ,μ)=(1,1)𝜆𝜇11(\lambda,\mu)=(1,1) for the subsequent finetuning stage in all four scenarios.
items.zh: 为保持一致性，我们在所有四种场景的后续微调阶段均使用(λ,μ)=(1,1)。

items.en: Pure LM performs similarly as no PM pre-training, which is unsurprising since it’s just an extension of the basic language model pre-training on which all our experiments are initialized.
items.zh: 纯LM的表现与无PM预训练相似，这并不意外，因为它只是我们所有实验初始化的基础语言模型预训练的扩展。

items.en: Pure PM improves sample efficiency for a small number of samples, but eventually underperforms relative to no PM pre-training.
items.zh: 纯PM在少量样本时提高了样本效率，但最终表现不如无PM预训练。

items.en: The PM+LM pre-training consistently improves sample efficiency relative to no PM pre-training. It also performs better than pure LM, thus indicating that the performance gain isn’t due purely to LM, but a combination of PM and LM.
items.zh: PM+LM预训练相对于无PM预训练持续提高了样本效率。它也优于纯LM，表明性能提升并非仅来自LM，而是PM和LM的结合。

items.en: What’s particularly interesting is that neither pure PM nor pure LM transfers particularly well, but the combined effort of PM+LM performs significantly better. Our hypothesis is that pure PM has a tendency to learn biased or “trivial” features (e.g., context length, token frequencies) that don’t generalize well to downstream tasks, while the addition of LM forces the PM to learn from more substantial “language-relevant” features.
items.zh: 特别有趣的是，纯PM和纯LM的迁移效果都不佳，但PM+LM的组合效果显著更好。我们的假设是，纯PM倾向于学习有偏或“琐碎”的特征（例如，上下文长度、词频），这些特征不能很好地泛化到下游任务，而加入LM迫使PM从更实质性的“语言相关”特征中学习。

full.sec: C.4 End-of-context Token Improves Preference Modeling Performance
full.secZh: C.4 上下文结束标记提升偏好建模性能

items.capEn: Figure 31: Appending an “end-of-context” token (EOC) to every sequence visibly improves overall performance, as seen here for both with and without PMP. In all cases where PMP is applied, we include the EOC token not just for the finetuning sequences but also the pre-training sequences. We made similar observations on all PMP datasets (as well as no PMP) and all finetuning datasets.
items.capZh: 图31：在每个序列末尾添加“上下文结束”标记（EOC）显著提高了整体性能，如图所示，无论是否使用PMP。在所有应用PMP的情况下，我们不仅在微调序列中，而且在预训练序列中都包含EOC标记。我们在所有PMP数据集（以及无PMP）和所有微调数据集上都观察到了类似的结果。

items.en: Here we outline a technical detail that improves the overall performance of preference models. We designate a special “end-of-context” token (EOC) which is included as the final token of each sample context. The preference model score is also predicted directly on top of this token. For our experiments we used the <SOS> token, but in principle many other choices are possible.
items.zh: 这里我们概述了一个提高偏好模型整体性能的技术细节。我们指定一个特殊的“上下文结束”标记（EOC），作为每个样本上下文的最后一个标记。偏好模型分数也直接在该标记上进行预测。在我们的实验中，我们使用了<SOS>标记，但原则上许多其他选择也是可能的。

items.en: We compare finetuning experiments with and without the EOC token. For experiments with, we consistently apply the same EOC token throughout both the PMP and fine-tuning stages; and for experiments without, we consistently do not apply the EOC token. From figure 31 we see that the EOC clearly improves performance.
items.zh: 我们比较了有和没有EOC标记的微调实验。对于有EOC的实验，我们在PMP和微调阶段始终应用相同的EOC标记；对于没有EOC的实验，我们始终不应用EOC标记。从图31中可以看出，EOC明显提高了性能。

items.en: We hypothesize that the improvement comes from two factors:
items.zh: 我们假设这种改进来自两个因素：

items.en: Sometimes the sentiment behind a natural language statement can be altered or reversed significantly by the addition of one or two words, and so knowing where the context ends can be helpful for the preference model to predict a sensible score.
items.zh: 有时，自然语言陈述背后的情感可能会因添加一两个词而发生显著改变或逆转，因此知道上下文在哪里结束有助于偏好模型预测合理的分数。

items.en: Without an EOC token, the preference model must not only predict a score, but also try to anticipate where the context ends. As a result, the model is forced to predict a score at multiple tokens where the context may end, rather than at a single token where it definitely ends. This adds a level of ambiguity which may cause the model to under-perform.
items.zh: 如果没有EOC标记，偏好模型不仅要预测分数，还要尝试预测上下文在哪里结束。因此，模型被迫在多个可能结束上下文的标记上预测分数，而不是在一个确定结束的标记上。这增加了模糊性，可能导致模型表现不佳。

items.en: In prinicple we can ensemble together several models finetuned on the same final dataset, but which first pass through PMP on a distinct dataset. This would be a bit like ensembling over different random initializations, but what might hope for more interesting results due to the different semantic content in distinct PMP distributions. We tested this for summarization PMs that were separately PMP trained on Reddit and Stack Exchange, but only found a gain of order 0.50.50.5% in accuracy.
items.zh: 原则上，我们可以集成多个在相同最终数据集上微调但首先在不同数据集上经过PMP的模型。这有点像集成不同随机初始化的模型，但由于不同PMP分布中的语义内容不同，我们可能希望得到更有趣的结果。我们在Reddit和Stack Exchange上分别对摘要PM进行了PMP训练，但仅发现准确率提高了约0.5%。

items.capEn: Figure 32: Accuracy gain of PMP as measured by accuracy difference relative to no PMP at 500 and 5k finetuning sequence pairs for multiple pre-training datasets (Mix, StackExchange, Reddit, Wikipedia) and finetuning evaluations (Learn to Summarize, HellaSwag, and all five Ethics evaluations).
items.capZh: 图32：PMP的准确率增益，以相对于无PMP在500和5k微调序列对上的准确率差异来衡量，涉及多个预训练数据集（Mix、StackExchange、Reddit、Wikipedia）和微调评估（Learn to Summarize、HellaSwag以及所有五个Ethics评估）。

full.sec: C.6 Experiments on Ranked vs Binary PMP – Synthetic Symbols Dataset
full.secZh: C.6 排序与二元PMP的实验——合成符号数据集

items.capEn: Figure 33: Results for a controlled experiment comparing the transfer ability of ranked vs. binary PM pre-training, as explained in section 4.3. We see a clear trend whereby the sample efficiency degrades as the amount of relative scrambling between pre-training and finetuning distributions increases. Furthermore, we find that binary pre-training does not transfer as well as the weakly scrambled case, but transfers better than the very strongly scrambled case, in agreement with our expectations. This possibly explains why binary pre-training seems to perform better than ranked pre-training in most of our finetuning experiments.
items.capZh: 图33：控制实验的结果，比较了排序与二值PM预训练的迁移能力，如第4.3节所述。我们观察到明显的趋势：随着预训练和微调分布之间相对打乱程度的增加，样本效率下降。此外，我们发现二值预训练的迁移效果不如弱打乱情况，但优于强打乱情况，这与我们的预期一致。这或许解释了为什么在大多数微调实验中，二值预训练似乎比排序预训练表现更好。

items.en: We began by generating a list of symbols, and assigned an arbitrary “Elo” ranking to them. A simple example would be the first five English letters ranked by alphabetical order.
items.zh: 我们首先生成一个符号列表，并为它们分配一个任意的“Elo”排名。一个简单的例子是按字母顺序排列的前五个英文字母。

items.en: We then generated a preference modeling dataset consisting of pairs of distinct symbols, so that within each pair a sample is “better” if it precedes the other with respect to the ranking. We call this the “control” dataset T0subscript𝑇0T_{0}. Furthermore, we created four additional datasets T1,T2,T3,T4subscript𝑇1subscript𝑇2subscript𝑇3subscript𝑇4T_{1},T_{2},T_{3},T_{4}, which were made in the same manner as T0subscript𝑇0T_{0} but with increasingly scrambled symbol rankings. For instance,
items.zh: 然后，我们生成一个偏好建模数据集，包含不同符号的对，使得每对中，如果一个符号在排名中先于另一个，则该样本“更好”。我们称此为“控制”数据集T0。此外，我们创建了四个额外的数据集T1、T2、T3、T4，它们以与T0相同的方式生成，但符号排名逐渐打乱。例如，

items.en: where we enclosed in square brackets symbols that are out-of-place compared to the control. In addition, we also created a “binary” dataset Tbsubscript𝑇𝑏T_{b} which labels symbols in the first half of the control ranking as “good” and those in the second half as “bad”. In other words,
items.zh: 其中我们用方括号括起了与控制组相比位置不当的符号。此外，我们还创建了一个“二元”数据集 T_b，将控制组排序前半部分的符号标记为“好”，后半部分的符号标记为“坏”。换句话说，

items.en: Finally, we pre-trained five preference models on Tb,T1,T2,T3,T4subscript𝑇𝑏subscript𝑇1subscript𝑇2subscript𝑇3subscript𝑇4T_{b},T_{1},T_{2},T_{3},T_{4} separately, and compared their finetuning performance on the control T0subscript𝑇0T_{0}. We also compare against a model trained directly on the control without preference model pre-training.
items.zh: 最后，我们分别在 T_b、T_1、T_2、T_3、T_4 上预训练了五个偏好模型，并比较了它们在控制组 T_0 上的微调性能。我们还与一个直接在控制组上训练、没有经过偏好模型预训练的模型进行了比较。

items.en: In our actual experiment, we found that using only five symbols was too “easy” of a task to clearly distinguish the performance of different models, so instead we created a longer list of symbols, but otherwise the idea is the same. See section C.6 for details. Figure 33 shows the pairwise comparison accuracy on a held-out test set vs. number of training samples during the finetuning stage. We make several observations:
items.zh: 在实际实验中，我们发现仅使用五个符号的任务过于“简单”，无法清晰区分不同模型的性能，因此我们创建了一个更长的符号列表，但思路相同。详见 C.6 节。图 33 显示了微调阶段，在保留测试集上的成对比较准确率随训练样本数量的变化。我们观察到以下几点：

items.en: We see a clear trend whereby sample efficiency consistently gets worse as the amount of scrambling increases. In fact, there is a scrambling “threshold” beyond which the sample efficiency is actually even worse than no PMP at all. This confirms the hypothesis that datasets with significantly different Elo scales are expected to transfer poorly to each other.
items.zh: 我们看到了一个明显的趋势：随着打乱程度的增加，样本效率持续下降。事实上，存在一个打乱“阈值”，超过该阈值后，样本效率甚至比完全没有 PMP 还要差。这证实了我们的假设：Elo 尺度差异显著的数据集之间迁移效果较差。

items.en: The binary dataset is similarly sample efficient as a “moderately” scrambled dataset. This agrees with our hypothesis, which posits that a binary dataset should transfer better than a strongly scrambled dataset, but not necessarily better than a weakly scrambled one.
items.zh: 二元数据集的样本效率与“中等”打乱的数据集相当。这与我们的假设一致，即二元数据集的迁移效果应优于强打乱数据集，但不一定优于弱打乱数据集。

items.en: Clearly, the best possible PMP dataset is one that is qualitatively very similar to the final finetuning dataset, but typically this is not available. We see binarized PMP as a compromise that cannot guarantee the best possible sample efficiency, but is more robustly capable of transferring to new preference modeling distributions.
items.zh: 显然，最佳的 PMP 数据集是在质量上与最终微调数据集非常相似的数据集，但通常这种数据集不可用。我们将二值化 PMP 视为一种折中方案，它不能保证最佳的样本效率，但能更稳健地迁移到新的偏好建模分布。

items.en: Finally, let us elaborate on our synthetic symbols dataset. Instead of using only five symbols, we used a list of 676 symbols (using all ordered pairs of uppercase English letters), with a randomly assigned ranking. For each symbol, the context is generated by repeating the symbol multiple times. For example, if the symbol AC precedes PQ in the ranking, then a preference modeling pair would look like
items.zh: 最后，让我们详细说明我们的合成符号数据集。我们使用了 676 个符号（所有大写英文字母的有序对），并随机分配了排序，而不是仅使用五个符号。对于每个符号，上下文通过重复该符号多次生成。例如，如果符号 AC 在排序中先于 PQ，那么一个偏好建模对将如下所示：

items.en: Furthermore, the scrambled datasets T1,T2,T3,T4subscript𝑇1subscript𝑇2subscript𝑇3subscript𝑇4T_{1},T_{2},T_{3},T_{4} were obtained by applying 10, 40, 160, 640 randomly generated transpositions to the control ranking, respectively. Finally, for the binary dataset Tbsubscript𝑇𝑏T_{b}, a symbol is labeled “good” if it appears in the first half of the control ranking, and “bad otherwise. For instance, if AC appears in the first half, and PQ appears in the second half, then the corresponding preference modeling pairs would look like
items.zh: 此外，打乱数据集 T_1、T_2、T_3、T_4 分别通过对控制组排序应用 10、40、160、640 次随机生成的置换得到。最后，对于二元数据集 T_b，如果一个符号出现在控制组排序的前半部分，则标记为“好”，否则标记为“坏”。例如，如果 AC 出现在前半部分，PQ 出现在后半部分，那么相应的偏好建模对将如下所示：

full.sec: Appendix D Per-Token GAN-Style Discriminator Results
full.secZh: 附录D 逐词元GAN风格判别器结果

items.capEn: Figure 34: Left: Discriminator and language model performance re-ranking Lambada answers. Right: Ensemble of discriminator and language model, as determined in equation D.2.
items.capZh: 图 34：左图：判别器和语言模型对 Lambada 答案进行重排序的性能。右图：根据方程 D.2 确定的判别器和语言模型的集成。

items.capEn: Figure 35: Per-token accuracy of a GAN-type discriminator trained to predict whether every individual token is human or model generated. Position 0 is the first model-generated token.
items.capZh: 图 35：GAN 型判别器在预测每个单独 token 是人工生成还是模型生成时的逐 token 准确率。位置 0 是第一个模型生成的 token。

items.en: One way to train a discriminator is to utilize pretrained language models to generate samples, and train the discriminator to distinguish between human and model generated tokens. The discriminator can then be used for rejection sampling, or ranking samples by how likely they were to be generated by a human.
items.zh: 训练判别器的一种方法是利用预训练语言模型生成样本，并训练判别器区分人工生成和模型生成的 token。然后，判别器可用于拒绝采样，或根据样本由人工生成的可能性进行排序。

items.en: To test out this naive setup, we created a training set by loading sequences of fixed numbers of tokens from the language pretraining dataset, followed by: with 1/3 chance truncating the text somewhere, and continuing the text by sampling from a language model; with 1/3 chance generating the same number of tokens entirely from a model; and with the last 1/3 chance the text remained unchanged (fully human-generated). We used a 13B language model for sampling this dataset. For training, we initialized discriminator models as pretrained language models, and applied a binary cross entropy loss at each token for the human vs. model binary classification.
items.zh: 为了测试这种简单设置，我们通过从语言预训练数据集中加载固定数量 token 的序列来创建训练集，然后：有 1/3 的概率在某处截断文本，并通过从语言模型中采样继续文本；有 1/3 的概率完全由模型生成相同数量的 token；最后 1/3 的概率文本保持不变（完全由人工生成）。我们使用 13B 语言模型来采样该数据集。训练时，我们将判别器模型初始化为预训练语言模型，并在每个 token 上应用二元交叉熵损失进行人工 vs 模型二元分类。

items.en: Although qualitatively the models seem to be able to identify low quality model generated text, when evaluated on a few language benchmarks, we did not see promising improvement over the original language model used to generate the training set (see figure 34). For these evaluations, we first generated 100 samples from each of the prompt in the test set. For the discriminators, we ranked the samples by the average predicted probability of being human generated over sample tokens. For the language model, we ranked the samples by the negative average log-prob over sample tokens. The plots show average metric over top-N ranked samples. We observe that the language model performs much better on these benchmarks, in both performance and robustness.
items.zh: 尽管定性上模型似乎能够识别低质量的模型生成文本，但在几个语言基准测试上评估时，我们没有看到相对于用于生成训练集的原始语言模型有显著的改进（见图 34）。在这些评估中，我们首先从测试集中的每个提示生成 100 个样本。对于判别器，我们根据样本 token 上预测为人工生成的平均概率对样本进行排序。对于语言模型，我们根据样本 token 上负平均对数概率对样本进行排序。图表显示了排名前 N 的样本的平均指标。我们观察到语言模型在这些基准测试上表现更好，无论是在性能还是鲁棒性方面。

items.en: However, we will now argue that it is not appropriate to directly use the discriminator to rank samples. Let us use P​(t)𝑃𝑡P(t) to denote the probability distribution of human-generated tokens and Pθ​(t)subscript𝑃𝜃𝑡P_{\theta}(t) to represent a language model. The goal of the discriminator Dϕsubscript𝐷italic-ϕD_{\phi} is to model the probability that a given token was model generated, so Dϕ​(t)subscript𝐷italic-ϕ𝑡D_{\phi}(t) is attempting to model the probability p​(human|t)𝑝conditionalhuman𝑡p(\mathrm{human}|t). Assuming a prior that a token is 50% likely to come from a human, after seeing the token, an ideal discriminator would predict
items.zh: 然而，我们现在将论证直接使用判别器对样本进行排序是不合适的。我们用 P(t) 表示人工生成 token 的概率分布，用 P_θ(t) 表示语言模型。判别器 D_φ 的目标是建模给定 token 由模型生成的概率，因此 D_φ(t) 试图建模 p(人工|t)。假设先验概率为 token 有 50% 的可能性来自人工，在看到 token 后，理想的判别器会预测

items.en: for the probability that any token was written by a human.
items.zh: 对于任何 token 由人工编写的概率。

items.en: But this means that we can use a learned Dϕ​(t)subscript𝐷italic-ϕ𝑡D_{\phi}(t) to improve model predictions for any given token t𝑡t, by re-arranging to give the new ensemble distribution
items.zh: 但这意味着我们可以利用学习到的 D_φ(t) 来改进模型对任何给定 token t 的预测，通过重新排列得到新的集成分布

items.en: and this ensemble model should improve on the original language model distribution Pθsubscript𝑃𝜃P_{\theta}. In particular, this ensemble provides a more principled way to re-rank model-generated samples, using both the discriminator and the language model probabilities together. We display the result on the right in figure 34, where we see that as expected, the ensemble can improve on the language model.
items.zh: 并且这个集成模型应该改进原始语言模型分布 P_θ。特别地，这个集成提供了一种更合理的方法来对模型生成的样本进行重排序，同时使用判别器和语言模型的概率。我们在图 34 的右侧展示了结果，可以看到正如预期的那样，集成可以改进语言模型。

items.capEn: Figure 36: We compare PMP on “human-human” vs “human-model” datasets by evaluating their transfer performance on the five Ethics datasets. It appears that one does not consistently outperform the other, and the results are rather random. We suspect that “human-model” does not have any particular advantage when finetuning on evaluations that are purely human-written, such as Ethics.
items.capZh: 图 36：我们通过评估在五个 Ethics 数据集上的迁移性能，比较了“人工-人工”和“人工-模型”数据集上的 PMP。似乎没有一个数据集始终优于另一个，结果相当随机。我们怀疑当微调评估数据完全由人工编写（如 Ethics）时，“人工-模型”并没有特别的优势。

items.capEn: Figure 37: Accuracy gain of binary over ranked PMP on finetuning evaluations.
items.capZh: 图 37：在微调评估中，二元 PMP 相对于排序 PMP 的准确率提升。

full.sec: Appendix E Definitions of Alignment and the HHH criteria
full.secZh: 附录E 对齐与HHH准则的定义

items.en: People often mean subtly different things when they talk about AI systems being "aligned". Given this, we want to elaborate on what we mean by this term and why we selected the "helpful, honest, and harmless" conception of aligned AI assistants.
items.zh: 人们在谈论 AI 系统“对齐”时，往往含义略有不同。鉴于此，我们想详细说明我们使用该术语的含义，以及为什么我们选择“有益、诚实且无害”作为对齐 AI 助手的理念。

full.sec: E.1 How the HHH criteria relate to alignment
full.secZh: E.1 HHH准则与对齐的关系

items.en: At a very high level, alignment can be thought of as the degree of overlap between the way two agents rank different outcomes. For example, if agent A completely internalizes the desires of agent B—i.e. the only desire A has is to see B’s desires satisfied—we could say that agent A is maximally aligned with agent B.202020Even if agent A is maximally aligned with agent B, A can fail to act in accordance with B’s desires because A has a mistaken belief about B’s desires or about the world, or because A is unable to carry out their intended action.
items.zh: 在非常高的层面上，对齐可以被视为两个智能体对不同结果排序的重叠程度。例如，如果智能体A完全内化了智能体B的愿望——即A的唯一愿望是看到B的愿望得到满足——我们可以说智能体A与智能体B最大程度地对齐。即使智能体A与智能体B最大程度地对齐，A也可能因为对B的愿望或世界有错误信念，或无法执行预期行动而无法按照B的愿望行事。

items.en: We believe it is difficult for an AI assistant to always be helpful, honest, and harmless towards an agent or group without also being highly aligned with that agent or group according to this definition of alignment. To see why, suppose we want the AI assistant to be aligned with a specific group of humans. Here is what each of the three conditions implies about the assistant:
items.zh: 我们认为，根据这一定义，AI助手要始终对某个智能体或群体保持有益、诚实和无害，而不与该智能体或群体高度对齐，是困难的。为了理解原因，假设我们希望AI助手与特定人类群体对齐。以下是三个条件各自对助手的要求：

items.en: Helpfulness: the assistant will always try to do what is in the humans’ best interests
items.zh: 有益性：助手将始终尝试做符合人类最佳利益的事情

items.en: Honesty: the assistant will always try to convey accurate information to the humans and will always try to avoid deceiving them212121This concept of ”honesty” involves avoiding multiple different conditions of lying, such as only stating true claims and not causing false beliefs in the listener [Mah15]. There will be cases where these conditions conflict. In such cases, we would need to assess which conception of honesty it would be most helpful and harmless for the assistant to satisfy.
items.zh: 诚实性：助手将始终尝试向人类传达准确信息，并始终避免欺骗他们。这一“诚实”概念涉及避免多种说谎条件，例如只陈述真实主张且不使听者产生错误信念。在某些情况下，这些条件可能冲突。此时，我们需要评估哪种诚实概念对助手来说最有益且最无害。

items.en: Harmlessness: the assistant will always try to avoid doing anything that harms the humans
items.zh: 无害性：助手将始终尝试避免做任何伤害人类的事情

items.en: An AI assistant that is always helpful, honest, and harmless towards a group of humans will always try to act in a way that satisfies the interests of this group, including their interest not to be harmed or be misled. It is therefore likely to be highly aligned with the interests of that group of humans.
items.zh: 一个始终对某人类群体有益、诚实和无害的AI助手，将始终尝试以符合该群体利益的方式行动，包括他们不受伤害或不被误导的利益。因此，它很可能与该人类群体的利益高度对齐。

items.en: This account of alignment is still vague and leaves many open questions. In particular, it does not tell us:
items.zh: 这种对齐的阐述仍然模糊，留下了许多未解决的问题。特别是，它没有告诉我们：

items.en: What kinds of outcome orderings are most relevant for AI alignment (preferences, idealized preferences, wellbeing, ethical rankings, etc.)
items.zh: 哪些类型的结果排序与AI对齐最相关（偏好、理想化偏好、福祉、伦理排序等）

items.en: The degree to which these outcome orderings are objective or subjective
items.zh: 这些结果排序在多大程度上是客观或主观的

items.en: Which agents the AI systems should be aligned to (users, developers, humanity, etc.)
items.zh: AI系统应该与哪些智能体对齐（用户、开发者、人类等）

items.en: How AI systems can or should aggregate different outcome orderings if they are aligned to more than one agent
items.zh: 如果AI系统与多个智能体对齐，它如何或应该聚合不同的结果排序

items.en: What is the precise formulation of "overlap between outcome rankings"
items.zh: “结果排序之间的重叠”的精确表述是什么

items.en: How large or small the space of maximally aligned agents is, given the above
items.zh: 在给定上述条件的情况下，最大程度对齐的智能体空间有多大

items.en: Many of these questions are discussed in more detail elsewhere [Gab20]. Progress in AI alignment will hopefully not require us to reach certainty about any of them, since such certainty is unlikely to be achieved. But it is worth making these unanswered questions explicit. When we train aligned AI systems, we may need to make choices that will implicitly favor or assume certain answers to them. And how we define the HHH criteria will depend on what kind of orderings we think are most relevant for AI alignment.
items.zh: 这些问题中的许多在其他地方有更详细的讨论。AI对齐的进展可能不需要我们对其中任何一个达成确定性，因为这种确定性不太可能实现。但值得明确这些未解答的问题。当我们训练对齐的AI系统时，我们可能需要做出选择，这些选择会隐含地偏向或假设某些答案。而我们如何定义HHH标准将取决于我们认为哪些排序与AI对齐最相关。

full.sec: E.2 The relation between the HHH criteria

items.en: If we define helpfulness and harmlessness such that (a) it’s never in a human’s best interest to be harmed, and (b) it’s always harmful to fail to do something that’s in a human’s best interest, we can reduce helpfulness and harmlessness to either criterion. We have separated them because we find it practically easier to distinguish cases of active harm from cases in which a benefit is withheld.
items.zh: 如果我们定义有益性和无害性，使得（a）受到伤害从来不符合人类的最佳利益，并且（b）未能做符合人类最佳利益的事情总是有害的，那么我们可以将有益性和无害性简化为任一标准。我们将它们分开，是因为我们发现区分主动伤害和未提供利益的情况在实践中更容易。

items.en: Helpfulness and harmlessness clearly can’t be reduced to honesty, but honesty can be reduced to helpfulness/harmlessness. According to the definition of alignment given above, an aligned AI assistant should be honest because honesty is valued by humans. This could either be because honesty is instrumentally valuable to humans or because humans intrinsically value it. If honesty were genuinely not something that humans value even on reflection, an AI that was aligned with human values would presumably not be honest.
items.zh: 有益性和无害性显然不能简化为诚实性，但诚实性可以简化为有益性/无害性。根据上述对齐定义，对齐的AI助手应该诚实，因为诚实被人类重视。这可能是因为诚实对人类具有工具性价值，或者因为人类内在重视它。如果诚实经过反思确实不是人类所珍视的，那么与人类价值观对齐的AI可能不会诚实。

items.en: But if the value of honesty is reducible to helpfulness and harmlessness, why include it in our list? The answer is mostly practical: honesty is important and distinct enough to warrant particular attention.
items.zh: 但如果诚实的价值可简化为有益性和无害性，为什么还要将其列入我们的列表？答案主要是实践性的：诚实足够重要且独特，值得特别关注。

items.en: We could also choose to introduce other concepts which—like honesty—can’t be reduced to helpfulness or harmlessness but are important properties that a helpful, harmless AI assistant will typically have. For example, we considered adding the following fourth ’H’:
items.zh: 我们也可以选择引入其他概念——像诚实一样——这些概念不能简化为有益性或无害性，但却是有益、无害的AI助手通常具有的重要属性。例如，我们考虑添加第四个“H”：

items.en: Handleable: the assistant will always be responsive to feedback from the humans and carry out any instructions from the humans in the way that the humans intended
items.zh: 可操控性：助手将始终对人类反馈做出响应，并以人类意图的方式执行人类的任何指令。可操控性与其他人所称的“可纠正性”类似。一个不可操控的系统比可操控的系统更不有益且更有害。但特别关注助手不按指令行事或不响应人类反馈的失败可能是有用的。因此，它似乎是第四个“H”的良好候选。

items.en: Handleability is similar what others have called "corrigibility" [SFAY15]. A system that isn’t handleable is less helpful and more harmful than a system that is handleable. But it may be useful to pay special attention to failures that involve the assistant not doing what is asked or not responding to human feedback. For this reason it seems like a good candidate for a fourth ’H’.
In other words, what we want to include in this list, beyond a joint or separate helpfulness/harmlessness condition, depends on what behaviors we find it useful to pay particular attention to.
items.zh: 换句话说，除了联合或单独的帮助性/无害性条件之外，我们想要在此列表中包含的内容取决于我们认为哪些行为值得特别关注。

full.sec: E.3 Conflicts between the HHH criteria

items.en: As we note in the main text, the three conditions above will sometimes appear to be in conflict. There are two possible kinds of conflicts between the three conditions:
items.zh: 正如我们在正文中指出的，上述三个条件有时会显得相互冲突。这三个条件之间可能存在两种冲突：

items.en: Intra-agent conflicts: Cases in which two or more HHH conditions are in conflict even if we just want to align the assistant with a particular human. For example, it is not possible to be honest or helpful towards a particular human without saying something that is pro tanto harmful to them, e.g. something that will hurt their feelings.
items.zh: 智能体内部冲突：即使我们只想让助手与某个特定人类对齐，两个或多个 HHH 条件也会发生冲突的情况。例如，在不对某个特定人类造成暂时性伤害（如伤害其感情）的情况下，不可能对其诚实或提供帮助。

items.en: Inter-agent conflicts: Cases in which two or more HHH conditions conflict across different agents we might want to align the assistant with. For example, it is not possible to be helpful towards a particular human without saying something that is harmful to a others we want to align it with, e.g. if one human asks for help building a bomb to use against others.
items.zh: 智能体间冲突：在我们可能希望助手与之对齐的不同智能体之间，两个或多个 HHH 条件发生冲突的情况。例如，在不伤害我们想要对齐的其他人的情况下，不可能对某个特定人类提供帮助，比如当一个人请求帮助制造炸弹来对付其他人时。

items.en: If helpfulness and harmlessness can be reduced to a single joint condition and honesty can also be reduced to helpfulness/harmlessness, intra-agent conflicts will turn out to be merely superficial since all three conditions can ultimately be reduced to a single coherent condition.
items.zh: 如果帮助性和无害性可以简化为一个联合条件，而诚实性也可以简化为帮助性/无害性，那么智能体内部冲突将仅仅是表面上的，因为所有三个条件最终都可以简化为一个一致的条件。

items.en: Inter-agent conflicts are a different matter. It is very likely that a single AI cannot be maximally aligned with any two different humans, since both humans will have at least some conflicting desires or values. This is why an AI assistant will often be unable to be helpful, honest and harmless towards some humans without being unaligned with other humans (e.g. by refusing to help them build the bomb). It is therefore important for us to be aware of who we are asking the AI assistants to be helpful, honest, and harmless towards, since this also determines which humans the AI assistants are not fully aligned with and to what degree.
items.zh: 智能体间冲突则是另一回事。一个 AI 很可能无法与任意两个不同的人类最大程度地对齐，因为这两个人类至少会有一些相互冲突的欲望或价值观。这就是为什么 AI 助手通常无法对某些人类做到帮助、诚实和无害，同时又不与其他人类对齐（例如，拒绝帮助他们制造炸弹）。因此，我们必须清楚我们要求 AI 助手对谁保持帮助、诚实和无害，因为这也决定了 AI 助手未与哪些人类完全对齐以及未对齐的程度。

full.sec: E.4 The HHH criteria and secure AI

items.en: Although we want to develop aligned AI systems, it may not be possible to guarantee that AI systems are fully aligned with human values. So we’ll also want our AI systems to be secure: to have properties or be embedded in systems that decrease the potential for harm even if the AI is less than fully aligned [HCSS21]. We may want AI systems that always respond to the intended instructions of humans, that always avoid doing certain things that most humans consider very bad, that fail both securely and loudly, that make decisions in ways that can be made transparent to humans, that are secure against alteration or misuse, and so on.
items.zh: 尽管我们希望开发对齐的 AI 系统，但可能无法保证 AI 系统完全与人类价值观对齐。因此，我们还需要我们的 AI 系统是安全的：即使 AI 未完全对齐，也要具备某些属性或嵌入到某些系统中，以减少潜在危害 [HCSS21]。我们可能希望 AI 系统始终响应人类的预期指令，始终避免做大多数人类认为非常糟糕的事情，以安全且明显的方式失败，以对人类透明的方式做出决策，防止被篡改或滥用，等等。

items.en: An AI assistant that is helpful, honest, and harmless is a secure system in many respects and will try to assist humans in making itself more secure. But the HHH criteria were not selected with AI security in mind and not all security features will be features of the AI system itself. We therefore want to emphasize that the HHH criteria are criteria of alignment, and that additional work and additional areas of focus may be required to ensure that AI systems cannot cause too much harm when they are not fully aligned.
items.zh: 一个帮助、诚实和无害的 AI 助手在许多方面是一个安全的系统，并将尝试协助人类使其自身更安全。但 HHH 标准并非为 AI 安全而选择，并非所有安全特性都是 AI 系统本身的特性。因此，我们想强调，HHH 标准是对齐的标准，可能需要额外的工作和额外的关注领域，以确保 AI 系统在未完全对齐时不会造成太大伤害。
